# CHANGELOG

- **ADR-087 web compaction state regression fix (`apps/api`, `docs`; 2026-05-11):** fixed the live web regression where `GET /api/v1/assistant/chats/web/:chatId/compaction` could return `503` even after successful compaction, which hid compaction/exhaustion banner state in the chat UI. The recent compaction-state refactor had switched the read path to `runtimeBundleDocument` but still parsed it as if it were an already-materialized object; in production that field is commonly a persisted JSON string, so shared compaction config resolved as invalid and the endpoint degraded to `runtime_degraded`. `ManageWebChatListService` now reads compaction config from `runtimeBundle` or `runtimeBundleDocument` and tolerates both object and JSON-document shapes; `BackgroundCompactionQueueService` now uses the same tolerant bundle parsing so queue notice classification does not drift from the web state path. Verification: focused `manage-web-chat-list.service` test, API lint, API typecheck, and prettier check on touched files are green.

- **Admin Ops user delete cleanup fix (`apps/api`, `docs`; 2026-05-11):** fixed the stale `/admin/ops` delete path so `AdminDeleteUserService` removes newer assistant/workspace-owned rows before deleting the assistant, workspace, and user (`assistant_files`, web turn attempts, media/background rows, knowledge chunks/retrieval rows, token/media counters, payment/billing history). The trigger-disabling helpers no longer mask the real failure with a secondary PostgreSQL `25P02` when an earlier statement aborts the transaction. Verification: focused `admin-delete-user.service` test and API typecheck are green.

- **ADR-093 proposed — clean PROD launch readiness and concurrency hardening umbrella (`docs`; 2026-05-11):** added [`docs/ADR/093-clean-prod-launch-readiness-and-concurrency-hardening.md`](docs/ADR/093-clean-prod-launch-readiness-and-concurrency-hardening.md) as the canonical **agent execution** program for large sessions (few deploys, mandatory intermediate audits, kubectl + short UI smoke after deploy, session handoff prompts for GPT-5.4, no business-logic shortcuts). Updated [`docs/TEST-PLAN.md`](docs/TEST-PLAN.md) with a top-level verification discipline section pointing at ADR-093 and the SR10 load-proof gate; updated [`docs/SESSION-HANDOFF.md`](docs/SESSION-HANDOFF.md) with an active umbrella pointer. **Documentation-only:** no claim that code-level PROD readiness or 500–1000 user ceilings are already proven.

- **ADR-087 deferred media enqueue guidance completion (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `docs`; 2026-05-11):** closed the last real media hard-limit seam that remained after the earlier quota-advisory/immediate-delivery pass. The direct monthly-media reserve path was already preserving `userFacingGuidance`, but the async/deferred media path (`POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/media-jobs/enqueue`) still short-circuited on quota precheck with only `{ code, message }`, so `image_generate` / `image_edit` / `video_generate` tool results could still surface `monthly_media_quota_exceeded` without the grounded package/upgrade guidance the model needed. `EnqueueRuntimeDeferredMediaJobService` now builds the same grounded copy through `QuotaGroundedLimitCopyService` when monthly media quota is exhausted, the internal enqueue controller/client now preserve optional `guidance` on `accepted=false`, and all three runtime media tool services now pass that `guidance` through on deferred-enqueue rejection just like the direct reserve path. Added focused regressions for the API precheck seam and the runtime image-generate deferred path. Verification: focused API/runtime tests, workspace lint, API/runtime/web typecheck green; repo-wide `format:check` currently fails on unrelated pre-existing drift in `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/infrastructure/persistence/prisma-assistant-memory-registry.repository.ts`.

- **ADR-087 quota follow-up immediate current-thread delivery + grounded media-limit guidance passthrough (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, `docs`; 2026-05-11):** finished the missing last mile between the already-correct notification architecture and the actual live chat behavior. `quota_advisory` still uses the same `source=quota_advisory` + policy `channels=["current_thread"]` path, but `NotificationDeliveryWorkerService` now exposes `deliverIntentNow(intentId)` so a just-created advisory can be delivered immediately through the existing notification pipeline instead of waiting for the background poll loop. `SendWebChatTurnService` and `StreamWebChatTurnService` now call that path right after creating the advisory intent, parse the delivered `web_thread:{chatId}:{messageId}` provider ref, and include `followUpAssistantMessageId` / `followUpAssistantMessage` in the returned transport so the active web chat can show the second message right away. `HandleInternalTelegramTurnService` now carries the advisory intent id forward, and `TelegramChannelAdapterService` triggers the same `deliverIntentNow()` only after the main reply is sent, preserving the correct order in the same Telegram thread without inventing a second delivery system. In parallel, the hard-limit media path now preserves grounded guidance end to end: `PersaiInternalApiClientService.reserveMonthlyMediaQuota()` reads `error.details.userFacingGuidance`, the runtime media tool result contracts (`image_generate`, `image_edit`, `video_generate`) now carry optional `guidance`, and the three runtime media-tool services include it on quota-skipped payloads. The media tool definitions were also strengthened so the model must use returned guidance and call `quota_status` for concrete package/upgrade options when a media tool is blocked by quota. Verification: focused API tests for quota-advisory + notification worker, workspace lint, format:check, and typecheck for API/runtime/runtime-contract/web all green.

- **ADR-087 quota/package truth hardening — durable advisory dedupe, hidden-plan free/paid truth, stricter runtime validation, and `/app/packages` stale-selection gating (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `apps/web`, `packages/runtime-contract`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** fixed the production audit findings left after the quota/package truth-unification slice. `QuotaAdvisoryFollowUpService` now stores `candidateDedupeKeys` on each created `quota_advisory` intent, and `ReadInternalRuntimeQuotaStatusService` now checks period-relevant follow-up intents against those per-candidate keys so repeated warnings in the same thread are durably marked `already_sent` even when the aggregate follow-up intent key differs or more than 24 hours have passed inside the same quota period. The same service now falls back to the actual plan catalog row and reads the real nested `billingProviderHints.presentation.price` shape when the current plan is hidden from public pricing, keeping `isFreePlan` and paid-light-mode eligibility honest for hidden zero-price plans. `QuotaGroundedLimitCopyService` now resolves upgrade guidance from the blocked tool's own offer truth instead of a global plan fallback. Runtime contract/validation was tightened so advisory candidates only expose the active warning-only delivery states and malformed `packagesPurchase` payloads are rejected. `/app/packages` now distinguishes `no_public_packages` from "tool disabled on current plan", disables checkout when shared package-purchase truth is unavailable, and clears stale selected package ids after plan-visibility refetches. Focused quota tests, repo lint, and API/web typecheck are green; repo-wide `format:check` still reports unrelated pre-existing drift outside this slice.

- **ADR-087 quota/package truth unification — concrete package offers in `quota_status`, warning-only follow-ups, and `/app/packages` on shared truth (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, `packages/runtime-contract`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** `ReadInternalRuntimeQuotaStatusService` and `ResolvePlanVisibilityService` now share one package-offer decision layer (`quota-offers.ts`) that computes per-tool concrete package rows, `offerableNow`, `preferredOfferKind`, preferred package ids, upgrade-plan candidates, and the canonical packages-page purchase link/methods. `quota_status` now carries that richer `packageOffers` payload through the runtime bridge and tool result, while `packagesAvailableByTool` is reduced to "buyable now" truth for the active assistant/tool context. `quota_advisory` candidates are now warning-only (`warning_90_percent`), so 100% hard-limit cases stay on inline grounded copy instead of generating second-message advisories. `QuotaGroundedLimitCopyService` now uses the shared package/upgrade truth to mention concrete package and plan options, `QuotaAdvisoryFollowUpService` passes the same package-offer facts into the runtime evaluation context, and `/app/packages` now reads `planVisibility.packageOffers` instead of maintaining a separate catalog + tool-availability decision path. OpenAPI/contracts were regenerated so `UserPlanVisibilityState` exposes `packageOffers`, and `AssistantBillingPaymentIntentPurpose` now formally includes `media_package_purchase`. Focused API/runtime tests plus contracts/api/runtime/web typecheck are green.

- **ADR-092 implementation slices 2+3 — payment-success email, receipt persistence, Admin notifications visibility, and recurring migration UX (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** `BillingLifecycleProducerService` now emits transactional success intents for `payment_activated` and `renewal_succeeded`, the billing template catalog gained branded payment-success templates and optional official-receipt footer links, and CloudPayments webhook processing now persists receipt URLs into payment-intent / billing-event metadata for later notification rendering. `ManageNotificationPlatformService` removes the old `workspaceId` scoping for platform-scoped notification admins, so `Delivery history` and active `Dead letters` are visible across workspaces for global `ops_admin` / `security_admin` / `super_admin`. `ManageAssistantPaymentIntentsService` and `HandleCloudpaymentsWebhookService` now persist explicit recurring-migration state for managed SBP upgrades (`in_progress` after real checkout readiness, `failed` with `provider_sbp_recurring_not_confirmed` when the one-time SBP payment succeeds but auto-renew still remains on card). Assistant Settings now surfaces explicit migration hints instead of silently collapsing to the generic billing hint. Focused API/web tests added for success templates, producer success intents, receipt persistence, migration state, notification visibility scope, and UI warnings.

- **ADR-092 implementation slice 1 — split last vs auto-renew payment truth (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** Prisma migration `20260510220000_adr092_split_billing_payment_truth` adds `workspace_subscriptions` columns for `last_payment_method_class`, `auto_renew_method_class`, recurring migration enum/status fields, and `provider_recurring_descriptor`. `AssistantBillingSubscriptionManagementState` replaces `paymentMethodLabel` with `lastPaymentMethodLabel`, `autoRenewMethodLabel`, and `recurringMigration`. `ManageAssistantBillingSubscriptionService` reads canonical classes, backfills default CloudPayments auto-renew as `card` when missing, and keeps heuristic last-payment labeling only as fallback when `last_payment_method_class` is unset. `HandleCloudpaymentsWebhookService` syncs instrument fields after applied lifecycle events (SBP managed upgrade keeps auto-renew `card` per ADR-092). `ApplyWorkspaceSubscriptionBillingEventService` passes `PersAI {displayName}` as CloudPayments `Description` on managed recurring updates and mirrors it to `provider_recurring_descriptor`. Web payment settings shows two tiles (last payment vs recurring charge). Tests and `DATA-MODEL.md` updated.

- **ADR-092 proposed — SBP recurring migration and split billing payment-method truth (`docs`; 2026-05-10):** added `docs/ADR/092-sbp-recurring-migration-and-billing-truth-split.md` (**Proposed**) as the executable architecture contract for PROD-only SBP auto-renew migration when the product promises it, explicit separation of **last payment** vs **auto-renew** method (forbidding long-term ambiguous combined labels), **provider-confirmed** recurring migration state, provider subscription **description** sync alongside amount/dates, expanded billing communications (payment-success + receipt link policy: PersAI-branded email with footer link to official provider/cash-register receipt), mandatory audit/acceptance for **Admin > Notifications** billing delivery history visibility, agent execution rules, and mandatory focused tests for future slices. Synced cross-links in `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/DATA-MODEL.md`, `docs/API-BOUNDARY.md`, `docs/TEST-PLAN.md` (new ADR-092 subsection), and `docs/ADR/084-billing-provider-readiness-pricing-checkout-and-payment-tools.md` continuation/relates list. No runtime or OpenAPI changes in this slice.

- **Billing Settings save step-up allowlist fix + quieter cancel-subscription billing copy (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** fixed the admin save blocker on `/admin/billing-settings`. Cluster logs in `persai-dev` showed the real failure shape: `GET /api/v1/admin/billing/lifecycle-settings` returned `200`, but the page then called `POST /api/v1/admin/step-up/challenge` with action `admin.billing_lifecycle_settings.update` and the controller rejected it with `400 action must be one of ...`, so the PUT save request never started. Root cause was `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/interface/http/admin-security.controller.ts`: `parseStepUpAction(...)` had not been updated when billing lifecycle settings got their own dangerous admin action, even though `AdminAuthorizationService`, the web client, and the OpenAPI contract already knew about it. The controller allowlist and error text now include `admin.billing_lifecycle_settings.update`, and `apps/api/test/admin-security.controller.test.ts` adds an explicit regression case for that action. In parallel, `Assistant Settings -> Payment settings` now uses calmer recurring-billing wording for paid subscribers: the CTA reads "Cancel subscription", the confirmation panel is visually quieter, the secondary action keeps the subscription instead of talking about auto-renew directly, and the success banner now states that the subscription ends after the current paid period rather than using infrastructure-flavored auto-renew phrasing.

- **Billing seed hardcode rollback + admin billing settings nav (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** rolled back the founder-rejected startup/manual-seed behavior that silently authored billing fallback policy. `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/seed-tool-catalog.service.ts` no longer backfills `lifecyclePolicy.trialFallbackPlanCode`, `lifecyclePolicy.paidFallbackPlanCode`, or `BillingLifecycleSettings.globalFallbackPlanCode`; it only preserves the original `starter_trial` bootstrap plus the bounded `runtimeTierDefault` backfill. `apps/api/prisma/seed.ts` keeps the safety merge into existing `billingProviderHints` so operator-owned JSON keys survive re-seeds, but it no longer defaults fallback plan codes to `"free"` when they are absent. `apps/api/test/seed-tool-catalog.test.ts` dropped the now-unused `planCatalogPlan.findUnique` / `billingLifecycleSettings` mocks. On the web side, `apps/web/app/admin/layout.tsx` now exposes `Billing Settings` in the admin sidebar immediately after `Plans`, pointing at the existing `/admin/billing-settings` page.

- **Trial-end fallback hardening (`apps/api`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** production discovered an expired-trial user (`strokovamasa7@gmail.com`) whose lazy `applyExpiredTrialFallback` was throwing `BadRequestException("Expired trial plan does not have a fallback plan.")` (HTTP 400) on every Ops Cockpit load because the `starter_trial` plan's `billingProviderHints.lifecyclePolicy.trialFallbackPlanCode` had been wiped (only `runtimeTierDefault` remained). The user stayed on `trialing` past `trialEndsAt`, never received the `billing.trial_expired` email, and the operator UI surfaced a hard 400 banner. Hardened the read path on three layers: (1) **`resolve-effective-subscription-state.service.ts`** — `applyExpiredTrialFallback` now resolves the fallback plan code via a 3-tier chain (`metadata.trialFallbackPlanCode` snapshot captured at trial start → current `plan.billingProviderHints.lifecyclePolicy.trialFallbackPlanCode` → `BillingLifecycleSettings.globalFallbackPlanCode`), and any remaining failure is caught at the `resolveWorkspaceSubscription` boundary with a `Logger.warn({ event: "expired_trial_fallback_skipped" })` so the cockpit cannot brick on a stale plan config. (2) **`seed-tool-catalog.service.ts`** — runtime auto-seed now backfills `lifecyclePolicy.trialFallbackPlanCode = "free"` and `lifecyclePolicy.paidFallbackPlanCode = "free"` on the default-registration plan when missing, and seeds `BillingLifecycleSettings.globalFallbackPlanCode = "free"` if the row exists with a null/empty value (operator-set values are never overwritten). (3) **`apps/api/prisma/seed.ts`** — the manual `prisma:seed` script now reads existing `billingProviderHints` and merges into it instead of overwriting with `{ schema, providerAgnostic }`, so re-running the seed cannot wipe `lifecyclePolicy`, `runtimeTierDefault`, presentation, or any other operator-edited extension. **Cluster data fix (persai-dev)** also applied directly: `starter_trial.lifecyclePolicy = { trialFallbackPlanCode: "free", paidFallbackPlanCode: "free" }` and `BillingLifecycleSettings.globalFallbackPlanCode = "free"`; the lazy migration then transitioned the affected user to `free`/`expired_fallback`, recorded both `trial_expired` and `fallback_applied` lifecycle events, and successfully delivered the `billing.trial_expired` email (`notification_intents.lifecycle_status = "delivered"`, provider ref captured).

- **Plan-level system-tool model slot UI (`apps/web`, `apps/api`, `packages/contracts`; 2026-05-10):** the admin plans editor now exposes the sixth model slot — "System tool" — alongside the existing Normal / Premium / Reasoning / Retrieval / Embedding rows, and the field is wired end to end. `AdminPlanInput`/`AdminPlanState` and the OpenAPI schema gained `systemToolModelKey`, `manage-admin-plans.service.ts` validates it via the existing model-key catalog and persists it under `billingHints.systemToolModelKey`, `materialize-assistant-published-version.service.ts` resolves the plan-level value (with the standard "stale → log warn and skip" guard) and forwards it to the routing resolver, and `resolve-runtime-provider-routing.service.ts` now prefers `planSystemToolModelKey` over the runtime provider profile primary model before falling back to the plan's normal-reply slot. The runtime-provider-routing test pins the plan-level override behavior. No DB migration was required because plan model keys live in `billingHints` JSON.

- **ADR-091 Session 3 — final audit and prod closeout (`apps/api`, `apps/sandbox`, `infra/helm`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** completed the mandatory final audit and moved ADR-091 from **Proposed** to **Accepted**. The Session 3 fix sweep made `PRISMA_CONNECTION_LIMIT` real runtime/deploy truth (`apps/api` now injects `connection_limit` into the Prisma datasource URL when the env var is set; `infra/helm/values.yaml` and `infra/helm/values-dev.yaml` now carry explicit defaults that match the documented pool-sizing rule), exposed `BackgroundSchedulerMetricsService` on the shared `/metrics` surface, and removed the last scheduler magic-number drift by naming/documenting the remaining cadence/batch/retry constants. Failure-model coverage now includes the missing explicit timeout/hang case from ADR-091 §G in the compaction scheduler suite. The audit also forced one unrelated but necessary Windows test-harness hardening in `apps/sandbox/test/sandbox.service.test.ts` so the full workspace gate reflects real regressions instead of `EBUSY` temp-dir cleanup/process-guard flake. Final verification gate: workspace lint, format:check, full workspace typecheck, full workspace test, `test:step2`, and build — all green. Known deferred follow-up remains the inherited idle `findDueCandidates()` holistic rewrite plus the compaction epoch-invalidates-leader footgun logged by the audit.

- **ADR-091 Session 2 — apply lease leadership to all four schedulers (`apps/api`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** completed the behavior slice that actually removes advisory-lock leadership from production scheduler paths. `PersaiIdleReengagementSchedulerService`, `PersaiBackgroundTaskSchedulerService`, `PersaiBackgroundCompactionSchedulerService`, and `AssistantMediaJobSchedulerService` now all follow the same lease-based tick contour from ADR-091: short `SchedulerLeaseService.acquire()`, periodic heartbeat while the leader drains work outside any outer transaction, idempotent `release()` in `finally`, and silent skip when another pod is leader. `BackgroundSchedulerMetricsService` is now wired into acquired / skipped / lease-lost / expired-recovered paths for all four schedulers. Idle re-engagement also moved to the ADR-091 throughput target (`batch=24`, `poll=5min`, repeated drain while leader) instead of the old single small batch. The legacy `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock` constants, comments, and wrappers were deleted in one pass. Tests now pin the new leader-skip and lease-loss drain-abort behavior across the scheduler suites, including a new focused background-task scheduler test file. Verification: workspace lint, format:check, API/web/runtime typecheck, full API test suite — all green.

- **ADR-091 Session 1 — lease foundation (`apps/api`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** landed the shared infrastructure slice for production-grade background scheduler leadership without touching scheduler behavior yet. Added `SchedulerLease` to Prisma plus migration `20260510140000_adr091_scheduler_leases` with seeded canonical rows for `idle_reengagement`, `background_task`, `background_compaction`, and `media_job`; added `scheduler-lease.constants.ts`; added `SchedulerLeaseService` with short-transaction `acquire` / `heartbeat` / `release` plus boot-time `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` seeding; added `BackgroundSchedulerMetricsService` with in-memory counter/histogram snapshotting for the seven ADR-091 metrics; wired both services into `WorkspaceManagementModule`; added focused tests for all five lease failure scenarios and metric recording; updated `ARCHITECTURE.md` with the lease-based scheduler paragraph plus explicit database pool sizing guidance (`PRISMA_CONNECTION_LIMIT`, `CONNECTIONS_PER_POD = max(10, cpu_count × 4)`). Verification: repo lint, format:check, API typecheck, full API test suite all green.

- **ADR-091 proposed + pre-ADR-090 inherited bug surgical fix in `findDueCandidates` (`docs/ADR/091-...`, `apps/api`; 2026-05-10):** authored `docs/ADR/091-production-grade-background-scheduler-architecture.md` — a single comprehensive ADR covering all four background schedulers (idle re-engagement, background tasks, background compaction, media jobs) for production-readiness at 1000+ users. The ADR specifies a universal lease-based single-leader pattern (`SchedulerLease` table + `SchedulerLeaseService` with acquire/heartbeat/release lifecycle, `LEASE_TTL_MS=90s`, `LEASE_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS=20s`) that completely replaces the ADR-090 `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock`-held-across-HTTP pattern, eliminating Prisma pool pinning during outbound LLM calls. Idle scheduler additionally gets drain-loop, batch=24, poll-interval=5min. Media jobs scheduler joins the unified pattern. Three sessions for sonnet 4.6 agents (lease foundation → apply to all four → mandatory final audit) with the audit step explicitly tasked with hunting for inherited bugs (any `findMany({ where: {} })`, N+1 patterns, in-JS filtering that should have been SQL, non-deterministic ordering columns, implicit cross-pod assumptions). No transitional/legacy modes. Status: **Proposed** until Session 3 audit completes. Separately, `findDueCandidates` in `persai-idle-reengagement-scheduler.service.ts` had a pre-ADR-090 inherited bug — `assistant.findMany({ where: {}, orderBy: updatedAt asc, take: 12 })` returned the 12 globally-oldest-updatedAt assistants without any idle filter, so 12 closed-marker assistants in the top of `updatedAt` order would starve every genuinely-idle assistant behind them tick after tick. Surgical fix replaces `where: {}` with a relational filter requiring at least one non-archived chat with a user message older than `idleHours`, narrowing the candidate pool to assistants that can possibly qualify. The holistic SQL rewrite (single qualifying-only query, eliminating the N+1 in the loop) is scoped under ADR-091 Session 2, not duplicated here. Verification: API typecheck, full repo lint, format:check, idle-scheduler test suite — all green.

- **ADR-090 final-audit hardening pass (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** third-pass audit (3 parallel readonly subagents) caught one production correctness bug and several quality regressions in the ADR-090 diff. (1) **`task.attemptCount` was stale after claim** — `claimDueTasks` writes `attemptCount = claimEpoch` to the DB but `claimed.push({ ...row })` was spreading `row.attemptCount` (the pre-claim value) into the in-memory `ClaimedBackgroundTask`, producing off-by-one semantics in every downstream check: `failTask` exhaustion (`task.attemptCount >= MAX_ATTEMPTS`) compared against the wrong number, retry backoff used the wrong attempt count, and `deferTask` rewound one attempt too far. Fix explicitly assigns `attemptCount: claimEpoch` after the spread, with a comment pinning the invariant. (2) **ADR-090 doc accuracy** — root-cause #2 narrative was wrong (claimed `externalThreadKey` was `task.id + scheduledRunAt`; actual key is `system:background-task:{task.id}`, `scheduledRunAt` only enters the receipt-key sha256); migration filename was `20260510120000_` (the directory was actually moved to `20260510130000_` to avoid an in-tree timestamp collision); files-changed list missed `apps/runtime/test/run-suite-isolated.ts`. All three corrected. (3) **Misleading test name** — `runEmptyAttemptIdRejectedTest` actually asserted _fallback_ (not rejection) to the legacy `externalThreadKey`. Renamed to `runEmptyAttemptIdFallsBackToLegacyKeyTest` everywhere (test file + isolated suite registry); changelog/handoff entries updated. (4) **Optional-chain hardening** — `buildContextPacket` was reading `assistant?.user.displayName`, `assistant?.workspace.locale`, `assistant?.workspace.timezone` — fully chained now (`assistant?.user?.displayName`, `assistant?.workspace?.locale`, `assistant?.workspace?.timezone`) so a future `select` change can't trigger an undefined-deref. (5) **Magic numbers extracted** — idle scheduler now defines `RUNTIME_SESSION_BUSY_DEFER_MS`, `SCHEDULER_TICK_TRANSACTION_TIMEOUT_MS`, `ERROR_BACKOFF_FALLBACK_MS`, `OPEN_LOOPS_CONTEXT_LIMIT` instead of inline literals; compaction scheduler defines `RETRY_MAX_DELAY_MS`. (6) **Compaction `releaseToPending` parity** — also sets `lastErrorMessage = "Released to pending: ${reason}"` so operators see the rewind cause alongside `lastErrorCode`, matching `deferJobAfterBusy`/`failJob`. (7) **Pool-vs-HTTP trade-off documented in code** — explicit comment block above each scheduler's `$transaction(...)` call explains the connection-pinning consequence of holding the advisory lock across runtime HTTP calls and the _correct_ future move (split lock into a short tx + lease row) instead of weakening the lock. (8) **Redundant cast removed** — `(result.pushText as string).trim()` replaced with a single `usablePushText: string | null` narrowed once at the decision branch. All verification gates green: workspace lint, format:check, API typecheck, runtime typecheck, full API test suite, full runtime test suite (with the renamed empty-attempt-id test).

- **ADR-090 post-audit hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** independent multi-agent audit drove a second-pass fix sweep on the ADR-090 diff. (1) **`deferTask` orphan run row fixed** — `persai-background-task-scheduler.deferTask` now wraps run-close + task-rewind in one `$transaction`: `assistant_background_task_run` is moved to `status="skipped"` with `errorCode/errorMessage`, and `attemptCount` is decremented (claim already incremented it for an attempt that turned out to be unconsumed). Eliminates infinite-running orphan run rows under 409 churn. (2) **Stale marker attempts on new-message reset** — idle scheduler `findDueCandidates` now treats `attemptsForCurrentUserMessage` as **0** when `marker.latestUserMessageAtSnapshot < latestUserMessageAt`; previously the prior-window count leaked into `runCount` and `recordEvaluation`'s increment math. (3) **Blank-`pushText` push no longer silently closes the window** — `decision==="push"` with empty `pushText` is now treated as `no_push` (increments attempts, warn-logs, last-decision marked `push_missing_text`); the previous behavior closed the marker for the entire idle window with no notification ever delivered. (4) **`patchPolicy` channels persistence fixed** — `input.channels` is now persisted on **both** the upsert create and update branches; was previously dropped silently after passing the new `IDLE_REENGAGEMENT_ALLOWED_CHANNELS` allow-list validation. (5) **`patchPolicy` config defaults preserved on create** — upsert-create branch falls back to `def.config` when `input.config` is omitted; first-touch upsert no longer wipes per-source defaults like `idle_reengagement.config.idleHours: 24`. (6) **Empty-string `evaluationAttemptId` defensive guard** — runtime `buildExternalThreadKey` helper now checks for non-empty after `.trim()`; prevents caller bugs from re-introducing the busy-conflict class via blank attempt ids. (7) **`outputSchema` in idle context packet now lists `push | no_push | complete`** (was `push | no_push`, mismatching `pushPolicyJson`). (8) **Magic constants extracted** — `RUNTIME_SESSION_BUSY_DEFER_MS`, `SCHEDULER_TICK_TRANSACTION_TIMEOUT_MS` in both task and compaction schedulers; advisory-lock id comments cross-reference each other. (9) **Tick error logs now include stack trace.** (10) **Compaction `deferJobAfterBusy` sets `lastErrorMessage`** for parity with the task scheduler. (11) Three new tests: `runPushWithoutTextDoesNotCloseMarkerTest`, `runStaleAttemptsResetOnNewMessageTest`, `runEmptyAttemptIdFallsBackToLegacyKeyTest` (originally `runEmptyAttemptIdRejectedTest`; renamed in the final-audit pass to match actual fallback behavior). All verification gates green.

- **ADR-090 Idle Re-Engagement Prod Hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** systematic fix for the OpenAI spam / parallel scheduler collision discovered from Cloud Logging (26 of 27 `runtime_turn_busy` events were `background-task-tool-run:...` idle evaluations fighting themselves). Five measures shipped together: (1) **`pg_try_advisory_xact_lock`** added to the `tick()` of all three schedulers (`idle_reengagement`, `background_task`, `background_compaction`) — only one API pod proceeds per tick, the other silently exits. Locks are xact-scoped and auto-release on commit/rollback. (2) **`AssistantIdleEvaluationMarker`** — new Prisma model (`apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`) + migration `20260510130000_adr090_idle_eval_marker` providing durable per-(assistant, chat) `latestUserMessageAtSnapshot` / `attemptsForCurrentUserMessage` / `nextEligibleEvaluationAt`. `findDueCandidates` now uses this as the primary cooldown and attempt-budget gate (`MAX_ATTEMPTS = 2`) instead of the fragile `notificationIntent.lifecycleStatus` filter that missed `failed`/`dead_letter` states. State transitions: `push`/`complete` → attempts=MAX; `no_push` → attempts++; 409 deferred → no increment; error → attempts++ with 30s/2min/10min backoff. (3) **Per-evaluation unique `externalThreadKey`** — `RuntimeBackgroundTaskEvaluationRequest.task` gains optional `evaluationAttemptId?: string`; the runtime uses it to build `system:background-task:{id}:{attemptId}` so each attempt runs in its own ephemeral synthetic session. The idle scheduler generates a fresh `uuid` per `evaluate()` call. (4) **HTTP 409 = `deferred` (not failure)** — `InternalRuntimeBackgroundTaskClientService` returns `{ ok: false, deferred: true, status: 409 }` for 409 responses; all three schedulers handle this without burning the attempt budget and reschedule +60s. (5) **Channel allow-list for `idle_reengagement`** — `patchPolicy()` now rejects `telegram_thread` and other non-idle channels with a 400, preventing the delivery-failure loop that fuelled re-evaluation. Tests: 6 new idle-scheduler tests (`push/cooldown/no_push/marker-exhausted/new-message-resets/409-defer`) and 2 new runtime tests (`unique externalThreadKey` / `legacy fallback`). All verification gates pass.

- **ADR-089 media-package highlighted flag + admin compact rows + user info hints (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** three founder-driven follow-ups landed together to bring `Admin > Plans > Media packages` and `/app/packages` to the founder-approved final state. (1) **Selected vs Highlighted are now separate concerns** — `MediaPackageCatalogItem` schema, types and admin/user surfaces drop the per-locale `badgeRu` / `badgeEn` text fields and add a single `highlighted: boolean` flag. Prisma migration `20260510120000_adr089_media_package_highlighted` adds `highlighted BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE` and drops the two `badge_*` columns. The new flag is admin-controlled and drives the gold gradient padding-box / gold gradient border on `/app/packages` (light) and a tinted gold border (dark), mirroring the highlighted-plan card on `/app/pricing`. (2) **User selection is back to a clear round checkmark** — `PackageChoiceRow` now shows a circular `Check` icon as the selection indicator (filled accent on selected) instead of the previous `ON / OFF` pill, and never co-opts the gold premium look for selection. Selected + highlighted compose cleanly via a thin accent ring, so an admin-highlighted preset still reads as premium even when the user has selected another row. The duplicate `10 единиц` subtitle is gone; the row now renders the optional admin-supplied subtitle from `item.subtitle.ru/.en` and hides the line entirely when both locales are empty. (3) **Admin block compressed to readable inventory rows** — `MediaPackagesSection.tsx` was rebuilt: per-type presets render as compact horizontal rows (`[10 u · 100 ₽ ★ … Edit Delete]`) packed up to 4 per row (`grid-cols-1 sm:2 lg:3 xl:4`), with `Edit` / `Delete` icon-only buttons (no oversized labels), and an `off` chip only for inactive presets. The form replaces the `Badge RU` / `Badge EN` text inputs with a single `Highlighted (gold premium border)` toggle and an `Active` toggle on the same row, both rendered as native premium toggles instead of native checkboxes. (4) **User packages page micro-hints** — each of the three media-type cards now ends with a single quiet `i` line: "Создаём изображение по описанию (промту)." / "Меняем готовое изображение по описанию." / "Создаём и редактируем видео по описанию." (en counterparts wired), so the user understands what a package is for without any extra noise. (5) **Stack adjustments**: `MediaPackageCatalogItemState`, `CreateMediaPackageCatalogItemInput`, `MediaPackageCatalogItem` (web client), and the admin POST/PATCH client signatures all drop `badge*` and add `highlighted`. `apps/api/test/read-internal-runtime-quota-status.service.test.ts` mock updated to the new shape. Verification: prisma generate, lint, format:check, api/web typecheck, full api test suite, full web test suite (341 passed), full runtime test suite.

- **ADR-089 quota_status purchase CTA + media UI polish (`apps/runtime`, `apps/web`, `packages/runtime-contract`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** three founder-driven follow-ups landed together. (1) **Settings monthly media cards** — `LimitMetricCard` now renders the value as `4 / 50 +10` (single line) instead of `4 / 60` plus a separate `включено: 50 тариф + 10 пакет` subline. The `bonusSubline` prop, the `formatMonthlyMediaBonusSubline` helper, and the `monthlyMediaBonusSubline` ru/en i18n key are all removed. The `осталось: 56` secondary line and the corner `Докупить` chip are kept. (2) **Packages page (`/app/packages`)** — `PackageChoiceRow` no longer shows the duplicate uppercase `10 ЕДИНИЦ` subtitle that just repeated the title. Selected state now uses the same gold gradient padding-box + gold gradient border on light theme and a thin gold border on dark theme as the highlighted-plan card on `/app/pricing`, plus an explicit `ON`/`OFF` pill so it's unambiguous which row is selected. The previous tiny circle indicator and the unused `Check` import are removed. (3) **`quota_status` tool gets an explicit purchase CTA** — `RuntimeQuotaStatusToolResult` now carries `packagesPurchase: { url, availableTools } | null`, populated by `RuntimeQuotaStatusToolService` whenever at least one entry of `packagesAvailableByTool` is `true`; the tool definition description in `native-tool-projection.ts` was extended to instruct the model: when the user asks anything about media limits/packages, call this tool first and, if `packagesPurchase != null`, plainly tell the user packages can be bought now and point them at `packagesPurchase.url` (default `/app/packages`) — explicitly forbidding the previous behaviour of saying "no checkout link was returned, packages may not be available". This is the bridge that fixes the founder-reported chat where the assistant kept giving piecemeal answers and never plainly directed the user to the packages page. Verification: lint, format:check, runtime/api/web/runtime-contract typecheck, runtime test suite (with new `packagesPurchase` assertions on success and failure paths), and the focused 30-test web `assistant-settings.test.tsx` suite.

- **ADR-089 LLM-authored background media-job failure copy (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** replaced the two canned phrases produced by `buildAssistantMediaJobFailureMessage` with LLM-authored, context-aware explanations on the failure path of background media jobs, so a job that fails (e.g. OpenAI moderation block on `image_generate`) is explained to the user in their own language with the actual provider reason — instead of always rendering one of two hardcoded sentences. Reused the existing success seam (`internalRuntimeMediaJobClient.complete(...)` + `RuntimeMediaJobCompletionService`) instead of building a parallel one: `RuntimeMediaJobCompletionRequest` now carries either `workerResult` (success, unchanged) or a new `failure` object (`code`, `message`, `attemptCount`, `maxAttempts`, `retryable`, `stage`); `RuntimeMediaJobCompletionService` branches prompt rules, payload, classification, request id prefix, and conversation thread key between success and failure modes. New `AssistantMediaJobCompletionTurnService.maybeFrameFailure(...)` mirrors the existing `maybeFrame(...)` and shares one private `loadFramingContext()` helper. `AssistantMediaJobSchedulerService.failJob` and `AssistantMediaJobCompletionDeliveryService.failDelivery` now try the LLM-authored copy first whenever a persisted user-message context is available and fall back to the existing localized hardcoded text when LLM framing returns `null` or throws (defensive fallback so the user is never silenced if the framing call itself fails). Provider-gateway classification list extended with `media_job_failure_explanation`. Verification: lint, format:check, runtime/api/web/runtime-contract typecheck, runtime test suite, api test suite (including new tests `uses LLM-authored failure copy when delivery fails and the framing call succeeds` and `falls back to hardcoded failure copy when LLM framing returns null`).

- **ADR-089 monthly media card redesign — corner chip instead of `+` watermark (`apps/web`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** rebuilt the monthly media limit cards in assistant settings after founder review. `LimitMetricCard` no longer renders the oversized translucent `+` watermark; instead the card stays fully clickable and shows a small premium corner chip in the bottom-right (`Докупить` / `Add packages`) that brightens on hover. The bonus subline is now explicit in both states: when a package bonus exists the row reads `включено: N тариф + M пакет` (accent tone), and when no bonus exists it reads `только тариф · можно докупить пакет` so the user always sees whether package capacity is currently active or only available to be added. New i18n keys: `settings.monthlyMediaPlanOnlySubline`, `settings.monthlyMediaBuyChip` (ru/en). Verification: `apps/web/app/app/_components/assistant-settings.test.tsx` (30 tests).

- **ADR-089 media-package checkout identity cleanup (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** removed the technical `__media_package__` / `MEDIA PACKAGE` strings from everything user-visible during a one-time package checkout. (1) **Bank/CloudPayments description fixed** — `cloudpayments-constructor-billing-provider.adapter.ts` now branches on `metadata.purpose === "media_package_purchase"` and sends `PersAI Пакет медиа N1/N2/N3 (фото/ред/видео)` instead of `PersAI subscription __MEDIA_PACKAGE__`, and uses payment button text `Оплатить пакет` instead of `Оплатить подписку`; subscription checkout description/button text unchanged. The numeric shortcode is built from the persisted `packageItems` (`image_generate / image_edit / video_generate` totals, in fixed order) so the bank statement and CloudPayments form clearly show what was bought. (2) **Our checkout page title fixed** — `apps/web/app/app/billing/checkout/[paymentIntentId]/page.tsx` now shows `Оплата пакета медиа` / `Pay for media package` for media-package intents, with a one-time-aware subtitle, instead of `Оплата тарифа MEDIA PACKAGE` plus subscription wording. Plan checkouts keep the original subtitle/title. (3) **i18n keys added** — `billingCheckout.titlePackage` and `billingCheckout.subtitlePackage` in `ru.json` and `en.json`. (4) **Focused regressions** — `apps/api/test/cloudpayments-constructor-billing-provider.adapter.test.ts` pins the new media-package description and button text, and `apps/web/app/app/billing/checkout/[paymentIntentId]/page.test.tsx` pins the new package title and one-time subtitle. Verification: adapter test, web checkout-page test.

- **ADR-089 media-package chat return banner cleanup (`apps/web`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** fixed the post-checkout chat banner so one-time media-package purchases no longer pretend to be subscription activations. `apps/web/app/app/_components/chat-area.tsx` now branches on the media-package sentinel (`__media_package__`) and uses package-specific `success / pending / failed` copy, plus routes failed package retries to `/app/packages` instead of `/app/pricing`. Added focused `chat-area` regressions for success copy and retry routing.

- **ADR-089 media-package checkout price label cleanup (`apps/web`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** removed the misleading recurring suffix from one-time media-package checkout pages. `apps/web/app/app/billing/checkout/[paymentIntentId]/page.tsx` now renders a plain formatted currency amount when the payment intent is a media-package purchase (`__media_package__` / `media_package_purchase`), while ordinary subscription checkouts still keep `/ month` or `/ year`. Added a focused checkout-page regression test covering both behaviors.

- **ADR-089 admin media package density fix (`apps/web`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** compressed `Admin > Plans > Media packages` preset cards after founder review. `MediaPackagesSection.tsx` no longer renders oversized full-width tiles; preset rows are now compact cards in a responsive grid (`1 / 2 / 4` columns depending on width) with tighter spacing, smaller metadata chips, and smaller `Edit` / `Delete` controls so the block reads like admin inventory instead of a user-facing landing page.

- **ADR-089 background media failure follow-up (`apps/api`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** fixed the silent web UX hole where a background media job could fail (including provider safety/policy blocks) and simply disappear from `activeMediaJobs` without any assistant explanation in chat. `AssistantMediaJobSchedulerService` now creates a user-visible assistant follow-up message on terminal scheduler failures, with safety-specific copy when the provider rejects the request under moderation/policy rules. `AssistantMediaJobCompletionDeliveryService` now also replaces the optimistic completion message with a failure explanation when delivery fails after `completion_pending`, instead of leaving an upbeat “ready” message beside a failed terminal job row. Added a shared failure-copy helper plus focused scheduler/delivery regressions. Verification: `apps/api/test/assistant-media-job-scheduler.service.test.ts`, `apps/api/test/assistant-media-job-completion-delivery.service.test.ts`, API typecheck.

- **ADR-089 quota_status media package truth fix (`apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** fixed the runtime bridge bug that made assistant/tool answers claim media packages were unavailable even when the package catalog existed. `ReadInternalRuntimeQuotaStatusService` in `apps/api` was already returning `packagesAvailableByTool`, but `PersaiInternalApiClientService.readQuotaStatus()` dropped that field during validation/projection, `RuntimeQuotaStatusToolService` omitted it from the `quota_status` payload, and `RuntimeQuotaStatusToolResult` had no contract slot for it. Result: the model could only see plan-owned monthly media limits and often answered as if separate purchases did not exist. The runtime contract, internal API client projection, tool payload, and focused runtime tests now all carry `packagesAvailableByTool`, and the native tool description explicitly mentions media package availability as part of `quota_status` truth. Verification: runtime typecheck and `apps/runtime/test/runtime-quota-status-tool.service.test.ts`.

- **ADR-089 media packages UX redesign round 2 (`apps/web`, `docs`; 2026-05-10):** rebuilt the founder-reported rough edges on the web/admin package surfaces instead of leaving the first-pass UI in place. (1) **`/app/packages` restructured into 3+1 cards** — three fixed media-type cards (`image_generate`, `image_edit`, `video_generate`) now sit beside one summary card in a single pricing-like grid, with one selectable preset row per type, clear checkmark state, and a single final Buy action. The decorative watermark drawings were removed from user package cards. (2) **Disabled-tool purchase guard** — the page now loads `getAssistantPlanVisibility()` and disables package selection for tools whose current plan has the tool turned off, showing an explicit upgrade-to-a-plan-with-this-tool hint instead of allowing a misleading purchase path. (3) **Assistant settings card is fully clickable** — monthly media limit tiles now use the whole card as the navigation target to `/app/packages`; the lower-right `+` watermark remains as a visual affordance, but the entire card is now the button. (4) **Right settings panel closes before navigation** — `AppShell` now passes an explicit `onOpenPackagesPage` callback so opening packages from assistant settings closes the slide-over first on desktop/mobile instead of loading the page behind it. (5) **Admin media package block simplified** — `MediaPackagesSection.tsx` package presets were compressed into readable utilitarian admin rows with units, price, order/currency badges, and visible `Edit` / `Delete` actions, removing the oversized decorative tile treatment. Verification: repo lint, format:check, web typecheck, focused assistant-settings test.

- **ADR-089 media packages CI/auth follow-up (`apps/api`, `docs`; 2026-05-09):** closed the next two regressions discovered immediately after the UI/auth cleanup landed. (1) **Admin plans media-package auth gap fixed** — `IdentityAccessModule` now also guards `GET/POST /api/v1/admin/plans/packages` and `PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/admin/plans/packages/:id`, so the `Admin > Plans > Media packages` block no longer renders `Authenticated user context is missing.` when listing or mutating presets. `identity-access.module.test.ts` now pins all four admin package routes. (2) **API suite non-CI-locality fix** — `apps/api/test/read-internal-runtime-quota-status.service.test.ts` now supplies the new `ManageMediaPackageCatalogService.listPublic()` dependency expected by `ReadInternalRuntimeQuotaStatusService` after ADR-089 added `packagesAvailableByTool`. Verification: `apps/api/test/identity-access.module.test.ts`, `apps/api/test/read-internal-runtime-quota-status.service.test.ts`, API typecheck, and a full `corepack pnpm run test` pass across the repo.

- **ADR-089 media packages UX/auth cleanup (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; 2026-05-09):** closed the founder-reported production regressions on the new media-package surfaces. (1) **Clerk auth gap fixed** — `IdentityAccessModule` now guards both `GET /api/v1/assistant/billing/packages/catalog` and `POST /api/v1/assistant/billing/packages/payment-intents`, so `/app/packages` no longer hits the controller with missing `req.resolvedAppUser` and the false `Authenticated user context is missing.` banner. `identity-access.module.test.ts` now pins both routes. (2) **Pricing-page residue removed** — the barely visible `Докупить медиа пакеты →` link was deleted from `pricing-page-view.tsx`; the package flow is now entered from the assistant-settings watermark/card path only. (3) **Packages page redesign** — `apps/web/app/app/packages/page.tsx` was rebuilt on the same layout system as the pricing page (`max-w-7xl`, centered hero, `rounded-[32px]` premium cards, full-width sections, themed tokens instead of ad hoc zinc colors). The page now reads like the tariff surface instead of a narrow custom block. (4) **Quota card clarity improved** — assistant settings monthly media cards now show `used / total`, a quiet included-capacity line (`included: X plan + Y pkg`), and an explicit remaining line (`left: N` / `осталось: N`). The buy watermark `+` stays in the lower-right area of the card, brightens on hover, and routes to `/app/packages`. Verification: `apps/api/test/identity-access.module.test.ts`, API typecheck, web typecheck, web lint.

- **ADR-089 media packages post-audit hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; 2026-05-09):** closed all 7 production-grade issues found by independent subagent audit. (1) Workstream 7 completed: `quota-grounded-limit-copy.service.ts` now reads `packagesAvailableByTool` and branches advisory copy between "buy a package", "upgrade plan", or both — monthly media exceeded copy also now uses `effectiveLimitUnits` for the used/limit display. (2) Idempotency: `WorkspaceMediaPackageGrant` gets `@@unique([paymentIntentId, packageCatalogItemId])`; migration `20260509210000_adr089_grant_idempotency` adds unique index; `fulfillPackagePaymentIntent` uses `upsert(update: {})` so duplicate webhooks are silent no-ops. (3) Webhook userId null guard: explicit throw instead of `?? ""` fallback. (4) Admin package cards show `ru / en` labels instead of RU-only. (5) Dead `packageType` prop removed from `PackageForm`. (6) Assistant-settings visibility includes tools with `effectiveLimitUnits > 0` even when `limitUnits === 0`. (7) Packages page expiry copy uses `t("monthlyMediaBonusExpiryHint")` i18n key instead of hardcoded strings. All verification gates pass.

- **ADR-089 Media package add-ons — full implementation (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, `docs`; billing/quota/ui 2026-05-09):** full end-to-end delivery of purchasable one-time media add-on packages for `image_generate`, `image_edit`, and `video_generate` tools. (1) **ADR-089 created** at `docs/ADR/089-media-package-add-ons.md` defining catalog/grant lifecycle, `base+bonus+effective` quota truth, payment intent purpose, currency constraints, and UI/UX rules. (2) **Prisma schema extended** with `MediaPackageType` and `MediaPackageGrantStatus` enums and two new models: `MediaPackageCatalogItem` (admin-configured presets with units/price/currency/labels) and `WorkspaceMediaPackageGrant` (per-purchase grant row linked to workspace and payment intent). Migration `20260509200000_adr089_media_packages` applied. (3) **Backend services added**: `ManageMediaPackageCatalogService` (admin CRUD for package presets), `ManageMediaPackagePurchaseService` (resolves active bonus units per tool, creates one-time payment intents with purpose `media_package_purchase`, fulfills intents by writing grant rows). `HandleCloudpaymentsWebhookService` updated to branch on `media_package_purchase` purpose and call fulfillment directly, bypassing subscription lifecycle. `WorkspaceManagementModule` updated. (4) **Quota truth overlay**: `TrackWorkspaceQuotaUsageService` now computes `baseLimitUnits`, `bonusLimitUnits`, `effectiveLimitUnits`, and `bonusExpiresAt` per tool; enforcement and percent calculations use `effectiveLimitUnits`. `ReadInternalRuntimeQuotaStatusService` exposes `packagesAvailableByTool`. (5) **OpenAPI contract updated** — `MonthlyMediaQuotaToolState` schema extended with `bonusLimitUnits`, `effectiveLimitUnits`, `bonusExpiresAt`; contracts regenerated. (6) **Admin UI** — `admin/plans/page.tsx` gets a second block via `MediaPackagesSection.tsx` for per-type (image gen / image edit / video gen) preset management with currency select (RUB/USD). Currency input for existing plans also converted to controlled select. (7) **User packages page** — new `/app/packages` route with multi-select premium cards, watermarks per type, live total price, single Buy button initiating one-time checkout. Subtle link added to `pricing-page-view.tsx`. (8) **Compact quota UI** in `assistant-settings.tsx`: `formatMonthlyMediaQuotaValue` and percent now use `effectiveLimitUnits`; new `formatMonthlyMediaBonusSubline` renders `X plan + Y pkg` subline in accent color when bonus > 0; `LimitMetricCard` extended with `bonusSubline`/`hasBonus` props and conditional accent border; quiet "Докупить медиа пакеты →" link below the grid routes to `/app/packages`. (9) **Stale code fixed** — orphaned `ForceReapplyAllSummary` type header restored in `assistant-api-client.ts` (previous session had split the type accidentally). All verification gates pass: lint, format (new files), API typecheck, web typecheck.

- **ADR-088 notifications admin auth + bilingual chat cleanup (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-09):** fixed the real Clerk wiring gap behind the founder-reported `Authenticated user context is missing.` failure on policy `Test` buttons and cleaned the redundant preview UX. `IdentityAccessModule` now registers both `GET /api/v1/admin/notifications/templates` and `POST /api/v1/admin/notifications/policies/:source/test` in `ClerkAuthMiddleware.forRoutes(...)`, so the new admin routes resolve `req.resolvedAppUser` like the rest of the notifications surface instead of bypassing auth middleware. `identity-access.module.test.ts` now pins both routes, and `admin-notifications.controller.authz.test.ts` now asserts `GET /templates` and `POST /policies/:source/test` stay admin-gated plus still surface 401 when no resolved user exists. On the web side, the `Preview / test send` block was removed entirely from `Admin > Notifications` (deleted `PreviewSection.tsx` and page section), leaving only real channel/policy test flows. The in-app notification-center thread title is now bilingual `Notifications | Уведомления`, and the adapter upgrades existing legacy English-only titles on the next delivery. Admin labels for `web_notification_center` now also show `Notifications / Уведомления` instead of English-only wording. Focused verification: identity-access route test, notifications authz test, API typecheck, web typecheck.

- **ADR-088 Notifications PROD Hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-09):** three correctness fixes and a premium email branding rebuild. (1) `user_preferred` routing corrected: `expandSemanticChannel` now returns `null` (not `web_notification_center`) when `preferred=telegram` but no active Telegram binding exists — the delivery worker escalates to policy escalation channel or marks intent `failed`; the silent implicit fallback is gone. "web" preference still correctly resolves to `web_notification_center`. (2) `testSendForSource` honest semantics: semantic channels are now expanded using the same logic as the real worker instead of being silently rerouted. `current_thread` returns a structured `failed` result with `reason: current_thread_requires_live_surface_context` and an explanatory hint. `user_preferred` looks up the admin's actual preferred channel and telegram binding, then uses the policy escalation channel or returns an honest `user_preferred_unavailable` error. `AssistantChannelBindingState` import added to the service. (3) Postmark template mode cleaned: `deliverWithTemplate` now sends only raw `factPayload` as `TemplateModel` (removed pre-rendered HTML/text variables), eliminating double-rendering when Postmark template override is active; `hasTemplate` guard now correctly rejects `0` and non-numeric strings. (4) Billing email HTML rebuilt with PersAI light premium design: warm sand background (`#f3ede6`), cream card (`#fefbf7`), 1px warm border, 12px radius, no heavy shadows, `-apple-system` font stack; footer with localized (ru/en) thank-you, `support@persai.dev`, `persai.dev/app` cabinet link, five footer links; plain-text version with full footer and URLs. (5) `TestSendBadge` UI component maps known error reason codes to human-readable labels and shows hint text as tooltip. All verification gates pass.

- **ADR-088 Notifications Admin PROD Cleanup — semantic channels + source-driven policies (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-09):** replaced raw-channel defaults with a source-driven semantic model, added Postmark TemplatedEmail, per-source test endpoint, and a rebuilt Policies admin UI. (1) Prisma migration `20260509110000_adr088_semantic_channels` adds `user_preferred` and `current_thread` to `NotificationChannelType` and upserts policy defaults with the new semantic channels. (2) Policy defaults rewritten: `idle_reengagement`/`reminder`/`background_task_push` → `channels=["user_preferred"]` with `escalationChannel=web_notification_center`; `quota_advisory` → `channels=["current_thread"]`; `billing_lifecycle` → `escalationChannel=admin_webhook` (was null). (3) `ResolveWorkspaceNotificationChannelsService` returns `{ available: true }` for semantic channels (no registry gate); `NotificationDeliveryWorkerService` calls `NotificationRoutingService.expandSemanticChannel()` before adapter selection; `user_preferred` fallback → escalation or `user_preferred_unavailable`; `current_thread` no-context → `current_thread_context_missing`. (4) `EmailChannelAdapter` gains `POST /email/withTemplate` (Postmark TemplatedEmail) mode: when `channelConfig.config.postmarkTemplateId` is set, sends via template; otherwise falls back to raw `POST /email`. Delivery worker merges `policySnapshot.config` into channel config so `postmarkTemplateId` stored on the `billing_lifecycle` policy reaches the adapter. (5) `quota-advisory-follow-up.service.ts` no longer hardcodes `allowedChannels`; passes `surface`+`chatId` for `current_thread` expansion. (6) New `POST /api/v1/admin/notifications/policies/:source/test` endpoint: builds deterministic demo facts for each source (billing event picker for `billing_lifecycle`), routes through the real pipeline including Postmark Template ID. (7) `PoliciesSection.tsx` rebuilt: `renderStrategy` read-only info with explanation, `channels` read-only list, per-source default/escalation channel dropdowns, Postmark Template ID only for `billing_lifecycle`, per-source Test button with billing event picker. (8) `ChannelRegistrySection.tsx` cleaned: email template picker removed (billing testing moved to Policies tab), channel-level Test is raw static_fallback ping, semantic channels filtered out. ADR-088 updated with semantic channel decisions and `grounded_llm` pass-through clarification. All 5 verification gates pass (lint, format:check, API typecheck, web typecheck, full API test suite).

- **ADR-088 email test template picker + collapsible channel settings (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-09):** follow-up operator UX hardening for `Admin > Notifications > Channels`. (1) `ChannelRegistrySection.tsx` now keeps channel-specific config and test controls behind a compact `Details` expander, so rows stay readable by default and advanced controls open downward only on demand. (2) Email test-send now supports choosing `Static message` or any live template from the backend registry, plus editable JSON fact payload for template-based email tests. (3) `TemplateRendererService` now registers the full billing template catalog (`trial_ending`, `trial_expired`, `renewal_failed`, `grace_ending`, `grace_expired`, `payment_recovered`, and `.short` variants) instead of only `billing.payment_recovered`, so template preview/test-send is no longer effectively single-template. (4) Added `GET /admin/notifications/templates`; `testSendChannel()` now accepts optional render input (`renderStrategy`, `templateId`, `renderInstructionRef`, `factPayload`) and routes the synthetic test intent through the matching renderer before adapter delivery. OpenAPI updated and contracts regenerated. Verification: lint, format:check, API typecheck, web typecheck, and `apps/api/test/admin-notifications.controller.authz.test.ts` all pass.

- **ADR-088 reminder + channel-ops polish (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-09):** follow-up operator-facing fixes after live testing. (1) Reminder leak fix: `handle-internal-cron-fire.service.ts` now strips the internal `Recent context:` snapshot from reminder `summary` before persisting `factPayload.pushText`; `build-reminder-context-snapshot.service.ts` exports `stripReminderContextSnapshot()` and `GroundedLlmRendererService` applies the same strip defensively, so Telegram reminders no longer show runtime grounding context to the user when the runtime echoes the full prompt. (2) Email config polish: `email-channel.adapter.ts` supports explicit `config.fromAddress` override in addition to `sendingDomain`, enabling a verified Postmark Sender Signature instead of the hard-coded `notifications@<sendingDomain>` fallback. (3) Admin channel UX: `ChannelRegistrySection.tsx` adds editable admin-webhook `endpointUrl`, email `fromAddress`, and email `sendingDomain`; after a successful test-send the backend clears `lastFailureAt`, resets `consecutiveFailures`, updates `lastDeliveryAt`, and the UI row updates immediately so stale red failure state does not linger after a healthy test. (4) Operator refresh UX: `DeliveryHistorySection.tsx` and `DeadLettersSection.tsx` add explicit `Refresh` buttons and 30s auto-refresh while the tab is visible, so new deliveries and dead-letter changes appear without re-running filters manually. All 5 verification gates pass (lint, format:check, API typecheck, web typecheck, API test suite).

- **ADR-088 PROD hardening round 2 (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-09):** four targeted fixes after live-cluster validation revealed remaining issues despite the prior audit pass. (1) Critical: `NotificationIntentService.createIntent()` dedupe lookup switched from `findFirst` filtered by `lifecycleStatus IN (pending, claimed, deferred_*)` to `findUnique` on the `(workspaceId, dedupeKey)` compound key. The previous filter missed rows that had already moved to `delivered` / `failed` / `skipped` / `dead_letter`, so a recurring producer (e.g. `PersaiIdleReengagementSchedulerService` with `dedupeKey` derived from a slow-changing `latestUserMessageAt`) collided on the unique constraint on every tick after the first delivery. Added a `try/catch` on `create()` that catches Prisma `P2002` and returns the winning row, defending against the race between two concurrent ticks. To re-fire intentionally, producers must include a fresh component (period, hour) in `dedupeKey` — both existing producers already do. Tests `notification-intent.service.test.ts` and `notification-delivery-worker.service.test.ts` updated to mock `findUnique` with the compound key. (2) Critical: Postmark token resolution. `EmailChannelAdapter` and `HandlePostmarkWebhookService` were calling `secretStore.resolveSecretValueByProviderKey(NOTIFICATION_CREDENTIAL_IDS.email_postmark[_webhook])`, but `NOTIFICATION_CREDENTIAL_IDS` constants are `secretId` values (`notification/email/postmark/api-key`), not provider keys — the wrong method bypassed the `PROVIDER_KEY_BY_SECRET_ID` mapping and returned `null`, producing `postmark_token_unavailable` in PROD even with operator-stored keys. Both call sites switched to `resolveSecretValueById`; `handle-postmark-webhook.service.test.ts` mock secret store now exposes `resolveSecretValueById`. (3) `ManageNotificationPlatformService.testSendChannel()` now resolves the admin's real assistant, first chat (`surface`, `surfaceThreadKey`, `chatId`), and email so adapters needing user-bound context can actually deliver instead of failing on missing fields; channel health row is updated post-send the same way the delivery worker updates it (delivered → reset failures + healthy; failed → increment failures + lastFailureAt) so operators see consistent telemetry. `listPolicies()` merges DB rows with `NOTIFICATION_POLICY_DEFAULTS` so all 7 sources are always editable; `patchPolicy()` switched from `update` to `upsert`. `listDeliveries` / `listDeadLetters` filter normalization (`isValidEnumValue` helper) silently ignores invalid `source`/`class`/`status`/`channel` values instead of crashing on Prisma validation. (4) UI: `DeliveryHistorySection`, `DeadLettersSection`, `PoliciesSection` — replaced free-text inputs for `source`/`class`/`status`/`channel`/`escalationChannel` with `<select>` dropdowns over the canonical enum lists; added "Clear" button. All 5 verification gates pass (lint, format:check, API typecheck, web typecheck, API test suite).

- **ADR-088 Post-audit production fixes (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-09):** six fixes applied after multi-agent audit of ADR-088 Slices 1–4. (1) Critical: `resolve-workspace-notification-channels.service.ts` — admin_webhook config key check was `webhookUrl`; corrected to `endpointUrl` to match adapter and UI; broken since Slice 4 landing. (2) Critical: `NotificationIntentService.createIntent()` now returns a `skipped` sentinel (never persisted) when `policy.enabled === false`; `NotificationDeliveryWorkerService.deliverIntent()` checks `policySnapshot["enabled"] === false` as a second guard for stale rows. (3) Critical: `admin-delete-user.service.ts` — workspace delete transaction now deletes `notificationDeadLetter` rows then `notificationIntent` rows before `workspace.delete`; `onDelete: Restrict` FKs would have caused transaction failure on last-member removal. (4) UI: `DeliveryHistorySection` and `DeadLettersSection` — filter inputs now debounced 450ms via `useState` + `setTimeout`; no more API call per keystroke. (5) UI: `DeliveryHistorySection` — bare `<>` fragments replaced with `<React.Fragment key={item.id}>` to eliminate React reconciliation warnings. (6) Defaults: `notification-defaults.ts` — `system_event.renderStrategy` corrected from `template` to `static_fallback` (matches migration SQL and producer); `exemptClasses` field removed from `NotificationQuietHoursDefault` type and value (was defined but never read at runtime). All 4 active verification gates pass (lint, format:check, API typecheck, web typecheck).

- **ADR-088 Slice 4 — operational migration + admin surface completion (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-09):** hard-cut of legacy admin notification path and admin surface completion. (1) New `SystemEventNotificationProducerService` replaces deleted `DeliverAdminSystemNotificationService`: maps audit event codes to `class=operational` (`assistant.runtime.apply_*`) or `class=administrative` (`admin.plan_*`), calls `NotificationIntentService.createIntent()` with `source=system_event`, `priority=immediate`, `renderStrategy=static_fallback`, `allowedChannels=["admin_webhook"]`, `respectQuietHours=false`, `traceId=auditEventId`. (2) Prisma migration `20260509100000_adr088_slice4_drop_legacy_admin_notifications` drops `admin_notification_deliveries`, `workspace_admin_notification_channels`, and three orphaned enums; upserts `system_event` policy row. `schema.prisma` cleaned of both models, 3 enums, and relations. `admin-delete-user.service.ts` cleaned of legacy notification cleanup. (3) `ManageNotificationPlatformService.testSendChannel()` wired to real channel adapters (synthetic intent, no DB write); controller returns `TestSendResult` with `@HttpCode(200)`. OpenAPI path and `TestSendNotificationChannelResponse` schema added; contracts regenerated. `assistant-api-client.ts` gains `testSendNotificationChannel()`. (4) `DeliveryHistorySection` adds `class` filter (server side was already wired). `ChannelRegistrySection` adds per-channel "Test" button with inline result badge, plus editable config fields for `web_push`/`mobile_push` slots (real adapters future ADR). (5) New test `system-event-notification-producer.service.test.ts` (10 assertions). All 5 verification gates pass; residue greps zero for all 7 legacy symbols.

- **ADR-088 Slice 2.5 closeout — multi-user correction hardened (`apps/api`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-09):** closed the seven NEEDS-FIX items + Clerk admin authz gap from the Slice 2.5 audit. (1) `BillingLifecycleProducerService` JSDoc no longer names the deleted `ScheduleBillingLifecycleNotificationsService`. (2) Inline `POLICY_DEFAULTS` / `DEFAULT_QUIET_HOURS` extracted to a single `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/notifications/defaults/notification-defaults.ts`; resolver, intent service, and worker import from one place; every `NotificationSource` / `NotificationChannelType` covered. (3) Resolver hardened: telegram requires `AssistantChannelSurfaceBinding.bindingState=active`; `web_thread` and `web_notification_center` are always available per workspace (registry row is advisory, never a gate); return type is now a discriminated `ChannelResolution` union with explicit `reason` (`auto_derive_unavailable` / `channel_disabled_globally` / `channel_unhealthy`) — every caller updated, no silent nulls. (4) New focused test `apps/api/test/resolve-workspace-notification-channels.service.test.ts` covers email/telegram/web/admin-webhook resolution and policy/quiet-hours fallback to defaults. (5) Schema decision: `notification_delivery_attempts` keeps no `workspaceId` column (workspace derived via `intentId` join in `ManageNotificationPlatformService.listDeliveries`); ADR-088 + DATA-MODEL.md amended to declare this explicitly. Less code churn, no migration. (6) DATA-MODEL.md rewritten to mark `notification_channel_registry` / `notification_policies` / `notification_quiet_hours` as global singleton tables, drop `billing_lifecycle_notification_jobs` from the live-table prose, and split per-event vs. global tables. (7) API-BOUNDARY.md documents the Postmark credential ids in tool-credentials and the `process.env`-fallback ban. (8) TEST-PLAN.md adds a Slice 2.5 subsection with run commands and a 5-rule interpretation table. (9) Authz gap on `POST /admin/notifications/channels/:channelType/test-send` closed: `ManageNotificationPlatformService.assertAdminAccess(userId)` runs the same gate as every other endpoint; new `apps/api/test/admin-notifications.controller.authz.test.ts` asserts every controller method rejects non-admin users with `ForbiddenException`. (10) Verified: `InternalNotificationsPostmarkWebhookController` is mounted at `api/v1/internal/notifications/postmark-webhook` and is NOT inside `IdentityAccessModule.forRoutes`, so Clerk middleware does not gate it (HMAC + dev-only bypass remains the gate); `apps/web/app/admin/layout.tsx` gates the entire `/admin/*` tree via `getAdminPlanVisibility`. All 5 verification gates pass (lint, format:check, API typecheck, web typecheck, full API test suite); all 14 residue greps return 0 across `apps/api/src` + `apps/web/app` + `packages/contracts`.

- **ADR-088 Slice 2.5 — Multi-user correction + notification platform PROD foundation (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-09):** fixed the per-workspace notification-config flaw in Slices 1–3 and delivered a PROD-grade global-singleton notification foundation. Key changes: (1) Prisma migration `20260509000000_adr088_global_notification_truth` aggregates per-workspace `notification_channel_registry` / `notification_policies` / `notification_quiet_hours` rows into global singletons (aborts on divergence), drops `workspace_id` column from the three config tables, adds global unique constraints; `notification_intents`, `notification_delivery_attempts`, `notification_dead_letters` keep `workspaceId`. (2) New `ResolveWorkspaceNotificationChannelsService` derives per-workspace channel availability at delivery time from `AppUser.email`, `AssistantChannelSurfaceBinding`, and global registry health. (3) `NotificationDeliveryWorkerService`, `NotificationIntentService`, `ManageNotificationPlatformService`, `HandlePostmarkWebhookService`, and `BillingLifecycleProducerService` updated to use global singletons. (4) Postmark Server Token and Webhook Token migrated to `Admin > Tools` credential store (`notification/email/postmark/api-key`, `notification/email/postmark/webhook-token`); `EmailChannelAdapter` and `HandlePostmarkWebhookService` resolve exclusively from `PlatformRuntimeProviderSecretStoreService`; env fallbacks removed. (5) `POSTMARK_SERVER_TOKEN`, `POSTMARK_WEBHOOK_TOKEN`, `POSTMARK_SENDER_DOMAIN` removed from `infra/helm/values-dev.yaml`. (6) `Admin > Tools`: new "Notifications" section with two Postmark credential cards. (7) `Admin > Notifications` rebuilt as compact operator surface: channel health strip, summary line, delivery history and dead letters collapsed by default. (8) `seedNotificationChannelRegistry` and workspace-loop deleted from `seed.ts`; seed writes zero notification rows. (9) Timezone-aware quiet-hours deferral fix in `NotificationRoutingService`. (10) ADR-088, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `SESSION-HANDOFF.md` updated. All 5 verification gates pass; residue greps zero in `apps/api/src` + `apps/web/app`.

- **ADR-088 Slice 3 — Transactional migration to unified notification platform with real Postmark email (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-08):** replaced `ScheduleBillingLifecycleNotificationsService` with `BillingLifecycleProducerService` calling `NotificationIntentService.createIntent` (class=transactional, renderStrategy=template, six billing rules, dedupeKey=rule:workspace:eventId, traceId=billing event id, respectQuietHours=false). Twelve TypeScript template modules (six full-form + six short-form) for `trial_ending`, `trial_expired`, `renewal_failed`, `grace_ending`, `grace_expired`, `payment_recovered` with ru/en localisation and unsubscribe footers. Prisma migration seeds `notification_policies` rows for `billing_lifecycle` per workspace, removes notification policy keys from `BillingLifecycleSettings.metadata`, and drops `billing_lifecycle_notification_jobs` table + two enum types. `BillingLifecycleNotificationJob` model removed from `schema.prisma`. Deleted `schedule-billing-lifecycle-notifications.service.ts`. `Admin > Billing Settings` lifecycle notification policy block replaced with link to Admin > Notifications. Legacy contract types `AdminBillingLifecycleNotificationCode/Rule/Policy` and `AdminOpsCockpitBillingNotificationJob` removed from `openapi.yaml` and generated contracts. `EmailChannelAdapter` routes billing emails to `factPayload.recipientEmail`. Added 2 new focused test files (`billing-templates.test.ts`, `billing-lifecycle-producer.service.test.ts`); extended `email-channel.adapter.test.ts`. All 5 verification gates pass; zero legacy symbol residue.

- **ADR-088 Slice 1 + Slice 2 — PROD cut: unified notification platform foundation + conversational migration (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-08):** combined commit closing all 11 items from the independent three-agent audit. Slice 1 (foundation): Prisma migration adds 9 enums and 6 tables; `NotificationIntentService`, `NotificationRoutingService`, `NotificationDeliveryWorkerService`; channel adapters (`telegram_thread`, `web_thread`, `web_notification_center`, `email`/Postmark, `admin_webhook`); structured logs with `traceId`/`userId`/`latencyMs`; full admin control plane API (channels, policies, quiet hours, deliveries, dead letters, preview); rebuilt Admin > Notifications UI with 6 dedicated section components; Postmark webhook HMAC security; escalation FK migration. Slice 2 (conversational migration): migrated `PersaiIdleReengagementSchedulerService`, `PersaiBackgroundTaskSchedulerService`, `HandleInternalCronFireService`, `QuotaAdvisoryFollowUpService` to `NotificationIntentService`; real conversational adapters (`TelegramThreadChannelAdapter`, `WebThreadChannelAdapter`, `WebNotificationCenterChannelAdapter`); dropped `assistant_notification_outbox`, `assistant_quota_advisory_states` tables; removed `whatsapp` from preference type; deleted legacy `PATCH /channels/webhook` from OpenAPI + contracts; stamped `traceId` across all 6 producers (ADR §11); removed `ManageAdminNotificationChannelsService` dead provider; surface-pinned `allowedChannels` on quota advisory intents; added 7 focused test files. All 11 audit fixes applied; lint, format:check (global), API+web typecheck, full API test suite, and 7 spot-run tests pass; zero legacy symbol residue in `apps/api/src` + `apps/web/app` + `packages/contracts`.

- **ADR-088 Slice 2 — Conversational migration (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-08):** deleted the legacy assistant-outbox path and migrated every assistant-authored conversational notification to the unified notification platform. Deleted services: `AssistantNotificationOutboxService`, `AssistantNotificationOutboxSchedulerService`, `AssistantNotificationDeliveryService`, `QuotaAdvisoryStateService`. Deleted tables (Prisma migration): `assistant_notification_outbox`, `assistant_quota_advisory_states`; migrated `workspace_notification_policies` (idle + quota rows) into `notification_policies`. Deleted enums: `AssistantNotificationOutboxSource`, `AssistantNotificationOutboxStatus`, `WorkspaceNotificationPolicySource`. Removed `whatsapp` from `AssistantPreferredNotificationChannel`.
