Producers migrated to `NotificationIntentService.createIntent`: `PersaiIdleReengagementSchedulerService` (skippable, grounded_llm), `PersaiBackgroundTaskSchedulerService` (immediate, grounded_llm), `HandleInternalCronFireService` (immediate, grounded_llm, respectQuietHours=false), `QuotaAdvisoryFollowUpService` (immediate, grounded_llm, pre-rendered pushText). `ScheduleBillingLifecycleNotificationsService` opportunistically migrated to transactional class intents. `GroundedLlmRendererService` short-circuits on `factPayload.pushText`. `TelegramThreadChannelAdapter`, `WebThreadChannelAdapter`, `WebNotificationCenterChannelAdapter` made real (no dry-run stubs). Legacy admin endpoints `GET/PATCH /admin/notifications/policies/idle-reengagement` and `GET/PATCH /admin/notifications/policies/quota-advisory` removed. `ManageAdminNotificationChannelsService` stripped to webhook-only. OpenAPI schemas, generated contracts, and web admin client cleaned of all legacy policy types and functions. Six focused API test files added/rewritten. Verification: lint, format:check (global), API typecheck, web typecheck, full API test suite all pass.

- **ADR-088 Slice 1 closeout — acceptance gaps closed (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, `docs`; notification platform 2026-05-08):** closed all Phase A acceptance gaps identified in the independent Slice 1 audit. Controller response shapes aligned with OpenAPI: all 6 affected endpoints now return bare views (no wrapper keys); `POST /dead-letters/:id/discard` returns HTTP 204; `GET /dead-letters` uses `deadLetters` key. Dead-letter replay now sets `resolvedAt = NOW()` and resets intent to `pending`; discard also sets `resolvedAt`; default list returns `resolvedAt IS NULL` rows only. Server-side channel filter moved from in-memory to SQL in `listDeliveries`. Dead-letters `GET` gains `source`, `status`, `dateFrom`, `dateTo`, `page`, `pageSize` query params; OpenAPI schema and generated contracts updated. Postmark webhook security hardened: unsigned requests rejected unless `POSTMARK_WEBHOOK_TOKEN` is unset AND env is `development`. Structured log events now carry `userId` + `outcome` fields; `latencyMs` measured from `intent.createdAt`. Admin UI refactored: `page.tsx` is a thin layout shell (< 100 lines); 5 new dedicated section components (`PoliciesSection`, `QuietHoursSection`, `DeliveryHistorySection`, `DeadLettersSection`, `PreviewSection`) each with loading/empty/error states, accessible markup, copy buttons, server-side pagination, and destructive confirmations. ADR §10 MJML note updated to TS modules; §Slice 1 stubs explicit. Escalation FK migration added. 6 new focused test files added and all pass. `DATA-MODEL.md` enum values corrected to match Prisma schema. `TEST-PLAN.md` ADR-088 section rewritten for actual coverage. Verification: lint, format:check (modified files), API typecheck, web typecheck all pass.

- **ADR-088 Slice 1 documentation completion (`docs`; notification platform 2026-05-08):** completed the `I-docs` documentation task for ADR-088 Slice 1. `docs/API-BOUNDARY.md` now lists all 11 new unified admin/internal notification endpoints and marks the 3 legacy endpoints as transitional. `docs/DATA-MODEL.md` now documents the 6 new notification tables, 9 new enums, and a legacy-table retirement schedule by slice. `docs/TEST-PLAN.md` now has a dedicated ADR-088 Slice 1 test section covering intent service, routing, worker, all channel adapters, postmark webhook, preview endpoint, admin page E2E intent, and legacy non-regression gate. `docs/SESSION-HANDOFF.md` updated with documentation sub-step. No code changes.

- **ADR-088 Slice 1 (Foundation) — unified notification platform core (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, `infra/helm`, docs; notification platform 2026-05-08):** implemented all ADR-088 Slice 1 scope. Prisma migration adds 9 enums and 6 tables (`notification_intents`, `notification_delivery_attempts`, `notification_channel_registry`, `notification_policies`, `notification_quiet_hours`, `notification_dead_letters`); legacy tables untouched. Application services: `NotificationIntentService` (single `createIntent()` entry point with quiet-hours deferral and deduplication), `NotificationRoutingService` (pure channel + escalation resolution), `NotificationDeliveryWorkerService` (claim/deliver/escalate/dead-letter polling worker), three render services (`grounded-llm-renderer`, `template-renderer` with `billing.payment_recovered`, `static-fallback-renderer`). Channel adapters: `telegram_thread`, `web_thread`, `web_notification_center`, `email` (Postmark), `admin_webhook`, plus stubs for `web_push` and `mobile_push`. Postmark email uses `POSTMARK_SERVER_TOKEN` / `POSTMARK_SENDER_DOMAIN`; bounce/complaint webhook ingress at `POST /api/v1/internal/notifications/postmark-webhook` with HMAC verification. Admin API: full unified `/api/v1/admin/notifications/` surface (channels CRUD, policies CRUD, quiet-hours CRUD, paginated deliveries, dead-letter management, renderer preview dry-run). `packages/contracts/openapi.yaml` extended with 15+ new schemas and 13 new paths; contracts regenerated; `assistant-api-client.ts` hand-rolled quota-advisory fetch replaced with generated client; all new unified notification wrapper functions added. Admin > Notifications page rebuilt with `ChannelRegistrySection` component showing unified channel registry. Channel registry seeded for `persai-dev` (5 channels). Verification: lint, format:check, API typecheck, web typecheck all pass. No production notification path uses the new platform yet.

- **ADR-088 hardening: no-residue rule and admin UI quality contract (`docs`, notification architecture 2026-05-08):** closed lazy escape hatches in ADR-088 so a slice cannot be marked done while leaving legacy notification code, contracts, endpoints, or UI blocks in the repo. Removed `or repurposed` from the legacy-table rule (legacy tables are dropped, never repurposed). Made principle 13 enumerative: each migration slice deletes the legacy database tables, legacy service classes (`AssistantNotificationOutboxService`, `AssistantNotificationOutboxSchedulerService`, `AssistantNotificationDeliveryService`, `DeliverAdminSystemNotificationService`, the assistant-push half of `ScheduleBillingLifecycleNotificationsService`, the direct chat-message write inside `QuotaAdvisoryFollowUpService`), legacy admin HTTP endpoints, legacy generated contracts/types, and legacy admin UI blocks it absorbs. Forbade feature flags/env toggles that keep legacy and unified paths alive in parallel. Added a UI quality contract for `Admin > Notifications`: dissolve the current single-file admin page into multi-component `_components/` shells in Slice 1; explicit loading/empty/error states; server-side pagination and filtering for delivery history and dead letters; one-click copy of intent id / dedupe key / trace id; accessible markup; generated contracts only. Locked surface convergence: notification policy block in `Admin > Billing Settings` and `latestNotificationJobs` card in `Admin > Ops` are deleted in their respective slices, not relinked.

- **ADR-088 PROD revision for unified notification platform (`docs`, notification architecture 2026-05-08):** rewrote `docs/ADR/088-unified-notification-platform-control-plane-and-delivery.md` from a vision document into an exec-ready PROD blueprint that a fresh agent session can implement without prior context. Added concrete Prisma target data model (`notification_intents`, `notification_delivery_attempts`, `notification_channel_registry`, `notification_policies`, `notification_quiet_hours`, `notification_dead_letters`) with normative enums; locked Postmark as the email provider with verified domain, MJML templates, bounce webhook ingress, and `List-Unsubscribe` headers; defined channel registry/capability matrix and dumb-adapter rule; added admin-configurable timezone-aware quiet hours with per-source opt-in (user reminders stay as a notification source but opt out of quiet hours and rate caps by default); added explicit escalation model with single-hop fallback, priority semantics (`immediate` / `scheduled` / `digest` / `skippable`), and structured-logs-first observability contract. Replaced the previous five-slice breakdown with four bounded slices ending with the legacy notification tables they absorb dropped. Removed `whatsapp` from `AssistantPreferredNotificationChannel` for Slice 2; promoted `system_event` source from dead enum to real producer hook in Slice 4. No code changes in this slice; subsequent slices implement the architecture against the now-stable target shape.

- **Revert premature dev image pin (`infra/helm`, docs; gitops correction 2026-05-08):** reverted the manual `global.images.tag` bump to `6e148950…` because that tag was not yet published to Artifact Registry and caused `api-migrate` `ErrImagePull`; automated CI is the normal owner of `values-dev` pin updates after images exist. Repo tag for dev is restored to `930ae9b9…` pending the pipeline.

- **Web quota-advisory DI hotfix (`apps/api`, docs; deploy/runtime hotfix 2026-05-08):** fixed the second live `persai-dev` `api` startup crash that remained after the Telegram follow-up hotfix. Both `SendWebChatTurnService` and `StreamWebChatTurnService` still accepted `QuotaAdvisoryFollowUpService` as `Pick<..., "maybeCreateFollowUp">` with an inline default object, which made Nest see the constructor token as `Object` and fail boot with `Nest can't resolve dependencies ... index [13]`. Both services now inject the real `QuotaAdvisoryFollowUpService` class, mark it `@Optional()` for manual tests, and use optional chaining with `null` fallback at the follow-up call site. Verification: `@persai/api` typecheck plus focused `send-web-chat-turn` and `stream-web-chat-turn` tests via `tsx`.

- **Telegram quota-advisory DI hotfix (`apps/api`, docs; deploy/runtime hotfix 2026-05-08):** fixed the live `persai-dev` `api` startup crash introduced by the ADR-087 Telegram follow-up wiring. `HandleInternalTelegramTurnService` had accepted the new quota-advisory dependency as `Pick<QuotaAdvisoryFollowUpService, "maybeCreateFollowUp">` with an inline default object, which made Nest see the constructor token as `Object` at runtime and fail module boot with `Nest can't resolve dependencies ... index [12]`. The service now injects the real `QuotaAdvisoryFollowUpService` class again, marks it `@Optional()` for manually constructed tests, and uses optional chaining with `null` fallback at the call site. Verification: `@persai/api` typecheck, focused `handle-internal-telegram-turn.service` test, and live `kubectl describe/logs` crash diagnosis against the failing pod.

- **ADR-088 unified notification platform architecture (`docs`, notification architecture 2026-05-08):** added `docs/ADR/088-unified-notification-platform-control-plane-and-delivery.md` as the new target-state ADR for one PersAI notification architecture across active-thread conversational warnings, billing lifecycle notifications, email, push, and admin/system alerts. The ADR records current repo truth instead of inventing a greenfield system: `assistant_notification_outbox`, `AssistantNotificationDeliveryService`, `billing_lifecycle_notification_jobs`, `workspace_notification_policies`, and admin webhook channels/deliveries are now documented as partial predecessors of a single notification control plane. `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `API-BOUNDARY.md`, `DATA-MODEL.md`, `TEST-PLAN.md`, and `SESSION-HANDOFF.md` were synced so ADR-088 is explicit target-state truth and `Admin > Notifications` is documented as the canonical future operator surface. Verification: doc review in this slice; repo-wide lint/format/typecheck/test gate follows separately in the same session.

- **ADR-087 strict cleanup follow-through (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; quota/runtime follow-through 2026-05-08):** a second stricter audit still found leftover parallel chat/error paths after the first Slice 4 cleanup, so the area was cleaned again instead of being declared done early. Telegram attachment-failure notes now actually reach runtime because `HandleInternalTelegramTurnService` passes `resolvedInboundMedia.enrichedMessage` into the native turn request instead of dropping it, and the dead `systemNotices` branch was removed. Web replay/reattach failed-terminal paths now surface recovered `status.error` / `onFailed` issues instead of silently cleaning up as terminal success. The stale in-chat `Fallback mode active` activity row was deleted so paid token fallback returns to the quiet sidebar-only ADR-087 marker, and `ChatArea` now renders storage issue `message` / `guidance` from server truth instead of replacing them with local storage banner text. Verification: API/web typecheck, focused `handle-internal-telegram-turn`, `render-assistant-inbound-surface-message`, `enforcement-points`, `use-chat`, and `chat-area` tests.
- **ADR-087 post-audit chat hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; quota/runtime follow-through 2026-05-08):** after a parallel multi-agent audit of Slice 4, closed the remaining chat-surface regressions instead of leaving them as “known tails.” Web pre-header quota hard stops no longer collapse into `send_failed` transport UX, because `use-chat` now treats quota/storage/auth/product conflicts as issue/banner-class failures before stream headers arrive. `assistant-api-client` now reads grounded `userFacingGuidance` from `ContractsApiError.payload`, so stream-error quota paths no longer fall back to stale local copy, and true `rate_limited` no longer renders as quota exhaustion. Telegram terminal hard stops now preserve and append grounded guidance from backend conflict details, the last Telegram-owned `token_budget_exhausted` override was removed, and the user-reachable inbound-media storage notice path was folded back into the normal assistant explanation flow. Verification: API/web typecheck, focused `assistant-api-client`, `use-chat`, `assistant-inbound-error`, `render-assistant-inbound-surface-message`, `internal-runtime-tool-quota.controller`, and `handle-internal-telegram-turn` tests.
- **ADR-087 slice 4 hard-stop unification + final cleanup (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; quota/runtime follow-through 2026-05-08):** finished the remaining founder-requested Slice 4 tail instead of leaving non-token 100% behavior split across old surface fallbacks. Added `QuotaGroundedLimitCopyService` so internal runtime monthly-media and tool-daily quota conflicts now build exhausted-limit copy from live `quota_status` truth, and web/Telegram surfaces now prefer those grounded server messages/guidance instead of replacing them with local hardcoded phrasing. Storage-limit cleanup landed in the same pass: inbound media notices now reuse canonical storage messages, `workspace_storage_bytes` is present in the shared quota bucket snapshot, and the legacy non-token fallback branches in `RenderAssistantInboundSurfaceMessageService` / `assistant-api-client` were trimmed back to server-truth passthrough. Verification: API/web typecheck, focused `handle-internal-telegram-turn`, `assistant-api-client`, `render-assistant-inbound-surface-message`, and `internal-runtime-tool-quota.controller` tests, plus workspace lint. `format:check` still fails only on pre-existing unrelated drift in `apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/telegram-bot.client.service.ts`, `apps/web/app/_components/app-url-open-bridge.tsx`, and `apps/web/app/app/_components/sidebar.tsx`.
- **ADR-087 slice 4 active-thread quota advisories (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; quota/runtime follow-through 2026-05-08):** landed the main active-surface delivery path for unified quota advisories instead of leaving Slice 4 as static policy copy. `QuotaAdvisoryFollowUpService` now evaluates real post-turn `quota_status` facts for the active thread, applies the new admin-owned `quota_advisory` policy instruction, and persists a second assistant-authored follow-up message only when durable advisory candidates are still eligible. Web sync/stream turns now return an optional `followUpAssistantMessage` in completed transport/status payloads so the open chat can append the warning immediately, and Telegram turns now return/send an optional `quotaAdvisoryMessage` as a second message in the same thread after the main reply. The admin notifications page now stores and edits a real `quota_advisory` workspace notification policy with enablement + LLM instruction, backed by a new Prisma enum migration for `workspace_notification_policies.source`. Verification: `@persai/api` typecheck, `@persai/web` typecheck, focused `send-web-chat-turn`, `stream-web-chat-turn`, `handle-internal-telegram-turn`, and `use-chat` tests.
- **ADR-087 slice 3 paid token light mode (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; quota/runtime follow-through 2026-05-08):** completed the paid token light-mode runtime/web slice behind ADR-087. `EnforceAssistantCapabilityAndQuotaService` now gates token-budget exhaustion by effective zero-price state: non-free plans continue via the existing safe `degrade_allowed` / `cost_driving_restricted` route until reset, while free/zero-price plans explicitly stop with `token_budget_exhausted` and do not silently enter paid light mode. The web app now surfaces the already-grounded `plan.advisories.tokenBudget.paidLightModeActive` state as a quiet `Light mode` marker in the left sidebar account footer and compact limits popover, with English/Russian locale copy synced. Verification: `@persai/api` typecheck, `@persai/web` typecheck, focused `enforcement-points` API regression, focused `sidebar` web test.
- **ADR-087 slice 2 quota truth + durable dedupe (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/contracts`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; quota/runtime follow-through 2026-05-08):** completed the quota/advisory groundwork slice behind ADR-087 instead of leaving half of it in docs. User-facing quota truth now carries advisory metadata directly from API source-of-truth objects: quota buckets, monthly media tool rows, and plan-visibility daily tool rows expose finite-limit, 90%-threshold, and reset-window state; plan visibility and internal/runtime `quota_status` now expose a shared `advisories` block with real plan facts (`isFreePlan`, higher paid upgrade availability, highest visible paid plan, token-budget reset window, paid-light-mode eligibility/activity). Slice 2 also added durable advisory persistence in `assistant_quota_advisory_states`, with thread-aware advisory candidate evaluation/dedupe for assistant-facing `quota_status` when runtime supplies active conversation context. Generated contracts and runtime quota-result types were regenerated/synced to the new shape. In the same slice, `EnforceAbuseRateLimitService` stopped treating token-budget usage as abuse-rate-limit pressure, so `rate_limited` returns to genuine abuse/spam protection instead of monthly budget exhaustion. Verification: contracts generate/typecheck, API/runtime/web typecheck, focused `plan-visibility`, `read-internal-runtime-quota-status`, `quota-advisory-state`, `enforce-abuse-rate-limit`, `runtime-quota-status-tool`, and `turn-execution` tests.
- **ADR-087 unified quota advisories and paid light mode (`docs`, product architecture 2026-05-08):** added `docs/ADR/087-unified-quota-advisories-and-paid-light-mode.md` and synced architecture/boundary/data-model/test-plan truth around one quota product model. The active target state now replaces token-budget slowdown/error sprawl with: assistant-authored 90% warnings in the active user surface for finite in-scope limits, durable per-chat advisory dedupe, paid token-budget light mode until period reset via the safe `cost_driving_restricted` path, warnings-only behavior for free/zero-price plans, quiet sidebar light-mode indication on web, and upgrade nudges only when a higher visible paid plan exists. Verification: doc audit only; no code/tests in this slice.
- **Admin notifications cleanup + ADR-087 operator block (`apps/web`, docs; admin UX 2026-05-08):** `Admin > Notifications` now exposes a dedicated compact `Quota advisories and light mode` block as the operator-facing home for ADR-087 policy truth, while the existing `Idle reengagement` editor was compressed into a much smaller, cleaner card. This starts ADR-087 Slice 1 without adding fake runtime behavior or legacy quota UI clutter. Verification: web typecheck pending in the current broader dirty tree.

- **Tailwind `dark` variant aligned with PersAI theme (`apps/web`, docs; UI/theming 2026-05-08):** Tailwind v4 defaults `dark:*` to `prefers-color-scheme`, while PersAI uses `html.light` + cookie for resolved light/dark (ADR-076). On Capacitor/mobile WebViews the OS scheme often disagreed with the in-app palette, so highlighted pricing cards could keep the light-theme gold fill during dark app theme. `globals.css` now overrides the `dark` custom variant to `html:not(.light)`, aligning `dark:*` utilities with document theme. Verification: `@persai/web` typecheck; focused `pricing-page-view` vitest.

- **Settings media availability truth fix (`apps/api`, docs; billing/quota UI follow-up 2026-05-08):** fixed the post-deploy seam where `Assistant Settings -> Limits & Plan` could still show the disabled video monthly quota card as `0/1` even though `video_generate` was OFF and the assistant-facing `quota_status` tool was already honest. Root cause: user plan visibility excluded monthly media tools from `limits.toolDailyLimits`, while the settings UI used that array as the availability source of truth for the monthly media cards. `ResolvePlanVisibilityService` now returns all `plan_managed` tools, including monthly media ones, with truthful `active` state; active non-media tools still resolve daily usage counts, while inactive/media entries stay visible for availability-only truth. Focused plan-visibility and assistant-settings tests plus API/web typecheck passed.

- **Public pricing page scroll fix (`apps/web`, docs; UI polish 2026-05-08):** removed the accidental contained-scroll mode from `app/pricing/page.tsx`, so `https://persai.dev/pricing` now uses normal page scrolling instead of rendering an inner scroll container whose scrollbar sat flush against the rightmost pricing card. The app-internal `/app/pricing` route remains unchanged. Verification: `@persai/web` typecheck.

- **`quota_status` billing-management parity (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; billing/tool follow-up 2026-05-08):** fixed the assistant-facing billing/tool seam where `quota_status` checkout creation still used the old raw payment-intent path and could wrongly refuse a real paid recurring upgrade (`Changing an existing recurring subscription in place is not supported yet...`) even though the main billing UI already used recurring-management truth. The internal quota checkout service now routes through `ManageAssistantBillingSubscriptionService.changePlan(...)`, so assistant-triggered paid upgrades create the same checkout contour as pricing/settings, while paid downgrade / `FREE` requests return an explicit `subscription_updated` outcome when the change was scheduled at period end instead of opening checkout. Runtime quota-tool result types now include `subscriptionUpdate` plus `action="subscription_updated"`, and focused API/runtime tests plus API/web/runtime typecheck passed.

- **Web chat limit banner minimalism polish (`apps/web`, docs; UI copy polish 2026-05-07):** removed the extra explanatory/detail line from the `chat_message_limit` banner so the limit card stays calmer and visually closer to the compaction banner. The message-limit state now renders only the title, the main action-oriented body copy, and the existing CTAs, while the rarer `active_chat_cap` banner keeps its technical-detail line. Verification: `@persai/web` typecheck.

- **Billing settings launch + admin FREE guard + live repair (`apps/web`, `apps/api`, docs; billing follow-up 2026-05-07):** closed the last founder-reported billing tails without reopening recurring-management scope. `AssistantSettings` / `AppShell` now close `Payment settings` before routing into bind checkout, so the 1-ruble recurring-enable checkout is no longer hidden behind the still-open settings slide-over. Scheduled `FREE` state in settings now renders as `Restore subscription` with matching success/bind feedback instead of the misleading generic `Enable auto-renew` copy. On the backend, both admin/manual paid-activation seams now reject zero-price `FREE` targets: `Apply workspace subscription` points operators to `Apply fallback now`, and Admin Ops `activate_paid_manually` can no longer create a corrupted paid-active `free` row with provider refs. Added focused web/API regressions, reran API/web typecheck, workspace lint, and `format:check`, and manually repaired the already-broken live `info@general-fly.com` workspace in `persai-dev` to the latest successful `basic` paid subscription state after confirming the real payment had landed but was ignored because of stale admin-written provider refs.

- **Web chat limit pre-header UX fix (`apps/web`, docs; mobile/web chat-limit regression 2026-05-07):** fixed the regression where a plan-owned `messagesPerChat` or `activeWebChatsLimit` refusal could land in the ADR-075 pending-send contour and show `Проверяем доставку` / `send_failed_unconfirmed` instead of the calm chat-limit banner that was intentionally added for this product slice. The `use-chat` pre-header stream failure path now classifies structured product errors first; `chat_message_limit_reached` and `active_chat_cap_reached` dismiss the optimistic local turn, clear the pending-send slot, and surface the existing banner issue state, while genuine pre-header transport failures still keep the old pending-send bubble/retry/cancel behavior. Added focused `use-chat` regressions for both limit classes before stream headers. Verification: focused `@persai/web` `use-chat` test, workspace lint, workspace format check, `@persai/web` typecheck, `@persai/api` typecheck.

- **PROD false media-claim honesty hardening (`apps/api`, docs; Telegram/web delivery honesty 2026-05-07):** fixed the remaining founder-reported seam where the assistant could say `Your image is ready`, `вот готовое фото`, or surface a legacy `Assistant sent an attachment ...` line even when the turn never produced a real artifact. The shared `final-delivery-honesty` utility now treats positive media/file-ready wording as a delivery claim and appends an explicit correction whenever no attachment was actually delivered, including the zero-tool-call / zero-artifact case that previously slipped through. `TelegramChannelAdapterService` now applies this correction after actual delivery outcome is known for every Telegram turn and persists the corrected text back into the saved assistant message, so false delivery claims do not remain in chat history for later hydration. Verification: focused `final-delivery-honesty` and `telegram-webhook-proxy.controller` tests, workspace lint, API/web typecheck. `format:check` still fails only on pre-existing unrelated web formatting drift in `apps/web/app/_components/landing-android-app-download.tsx`, `apps/web/app/app/_components/sidebar.tsx`, and `apps/web/app/app/_components/sidebar.test.tsx`.

- **ADR-081 Working Files prompt assembly hardening (`apps/runtime`, docs; live runtime regression 2026-05-07):** replaced the mutable string-based `Working Files` refresh path in `TurnExecutionService` with deterministic typed developer-instruction sections that are rendered fresh on each loop instead of regex-splicing one block into an already-rendered prompt. This removes the root seam that could duplicate `## Working Files`, clobber adjacent sections such as `# Sense of Time` / `## Open Media Jobs`, and keep unstable developer prompt state across tool loops. The model-visible `Working Files` section is now hard-capped to the latest 20 entries, and its guidance no longer prints raw `fileRef` / `artifactId` / `objectKey` / `attachmentId` vocabulary back into the prompt. In the same live-regression fix, runtime now strips legacy technical attachment-summary lines such as `Assistant sent an attachment ... fileRef ...` before reinjecting accumulated assistant text into the next tool-loop provider call, so the model cannot self-condition on stale technical delivery text mid-turn. Verification: focused `working-files-developer-section` and `turn-execution.service` runtime tests, `@persai/runtime` typecheck, workspace lint, `@persai/api` typecheck, `@persai/web` typecheck. `format:check` now fails only on the same pre-existing unrelated web drift in `apps/web/app/_components/landing-android-app-download.tsx`, `apps/web/app/app/_components/sidebar.tsx`, and `apps/web/app/app/_components/sidebar.test.tsx`.

- **Chat-length plan policy + calm web gating (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/contracts`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; chat UX / plan policy 2026-05-07):** added plan-level `messagesPerChat` across admin plan write/read models, generated contracts, and runtime quota-status visible-plan metadata; normalized `activeWebChatsLimit` to internal-only `0 = unlimited` semantics instead of treating `0` as invalid/missing truth; and enforced the new per-chat length limit before persisting the next user web-chat message. The web product surface now hides `active web chats` from pricing facts, maps both `chat_message_limit_reached` and rare `active_chat_cap_reached` into calm above-composer banners with a soft pricing CTA, and keeps new-thread creation honest by failing before a new durable chat/message is committed. `Admin > Plans` now exposes `Messages per chat` alongside the internal active-chat field, and `Admin > Ops` replaces the old active-chat quota bar with compact per-tool monthly media usage rows. Verification: contracts generation/typecheck, focused `prepare-assistant-inbound-turn`, `resolve-admin-ops-cockpit`, `runtime-quota-status-tool`, pricing/admin-ops/admin-plans web tests, workspace lint, API/runtime/web typecheck, full workspace `pnpm run test`. `format:check` still fails only on pre-existing unrelated drift in `apps/web/app/_components/landing-android-app-download.tsx`, `apps/web/app/app/_components/sidebar.tsx`, and `apps/web/app/app/_components/sidebar.test.tsx`.

- **ADR-084 billing mutation auth route fix (`apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-07):** corrected the actual founder-reported production auth seam after re-checking live `persai-dev` logs against the current code instead of assuming the previous proxy hypothesis was sufficient. The decisive pattern was stable: `GET /api/v1/assistant/billing/subscription` resolved a real `userId`, while the immediately following `POST /api/v1/assistant/billing/subscription/enable-auto-renew` still returned `401 userId=null` and `api` emitted no Clerk token-verification warnings. Root cause: `IdentityAccessModule` registered Clerk middleware for billing reads, payment intents, and `disable-auto-renew`, but omitted the two new recurring-management mutation routes `POST /assistant/billing/subscription/enable-auto-renew` and `POST /assistant/billing/subscription/change-plan`. Those requests therefore reached `AssistantBillingController` without `req.resolvedAppUser` and were rejected as `Authenticated user context is missing.` in ~1ms despite a live session. Added both routes to the `ClerkAuthMiddleware` allowlist and extended the existing identity-access regression so future billing route additions fail loudly if they are not wired into auth. Verification: focused `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/identity-access.module.test.ts`, workspace lint, `@persai/api` typecheck, `@persai/web` typecheck. `corepack pnpm run format:check` still fails on pre-existing unrelated web formatting drift in `apps/web/app/_components/landing-android-app-download.tsx`, `apps/web/app/app/_components/sidebar.tsx`, and `apps/web/app/app/_components/sidebar.test.tsx`.

- **ADR-081 async media alias + developer-block follow-up (`apps/runtime`, `apps/api`, docs; live regression 2026-05-07):** fixed the live post-ADR-081 regression where deferred media jobs (`image_edit` / `video_generate`) could be enqueued and acknowledged but then fail without ever delivering artifacts. Live `persai-dev` inspection showed the async media rows were created and claimed, but the runtime worker returned zero artifacts and the jobs later died as `completion_artifacts_missing`. Root cause: the internal runtime `/media-jobs/run` parser dropped attachment `aliases`, so async media workers lost alias-based source/reference image resolution even though the foreground path still worked. The runtime controller now preserves attachment aliases across the internal media-job seam, and the API media-job scheduler now fails zero-artifact runtime runs immediately with `media_job_artifacts_missing` instead of promoting them to `completion_pending`. In the same follow-up, the `Working Files` developer-block refresh regex was fixed to stop at any markdown heading level, which preserves adjacent `Sense of Time` / `Open Media Jobs` sections and avoids unstable duplicate alias-block refresh behavior across tool loops. Verification: focused `runtime-media-request-parsing`, `working-files-developer-section`, and `assistant-media-job-scheduler.service` tests, plus live `kubectl` / Prisma inspection and a raw internal rerun of a failed media job in `persai-dev`.

- **ADR-084 billing POST auth seam fix (`apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-07):** fixed the still-live cluster seam where billing reads succeeded but billing mutations (`enable-auto-renew`, `change-plan`) were still reaching `api` as `401 userId=null`. Live `persai-dev` logs showed that the earlier `clerkMiddleware()` matcher bug was gone, but the web proxy could still forward a stale/invalid browser bearer header even while a valid same-origin Clerk cookie session existed. `app/api/v1/[[...path]]/route.ts` now prefers a fresh server-side Clerk bearer from `auth().getToken()` whenever one is available and only preserves the incoming `Authorization` header as a fallback when no server session token exists. Focused proxy regressions were added for both branches, and focused billing web tests plus workspace lint and API/web typecheck passed. `format:check` still reports a pre-existing unrelated drift in `apps/runtime/test/turn-context-hydration.service.test.ts`.

- **ADR-081 attachment-context alias split (`apps/runtime`, `apps/api`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; attachment/runtime hygiene 2026-05-07):** fixed the production attachment-context leak where runtime hydration and tool-result follow-up were exposing raw `fileRef`/technical attachment summaries to the model as if they were normal chat text. Model-visible history no longer injects `Assistant sent an attachment ... fileRef ...` or `[Working files from user attachments ...]` blocks, runtime now builds a server-owned `Working Files` alias section in developer instructions, and reusable file/media selection for `files`, `image_edit`, and `video_generate` is now alias-first (`current image #1`, `previous attachment #1`, `last generated image`, etc.) with deterministic runtime resolution back to canonical `fileRef`. The post-audit polish then removed the remaining PROD tails: non-send `files` tool results now also hide raw selector fields from model-visible tool history, registry search no longer matches raw UUID/object-key selectors, plural `files.send` alias lists fail honestly instead of partial-sending, media alias inference now reuses real attachment aliases/provenance instead of hardcoded current-image assumptions, and the stale hydration helper block that still formatted legacy `Assistant sent ... fileRef ...` text was deleted. Tool-catalog/projection guidance and focused regressions were aligned to the alias-first contract. Verification: focused `turn-context-hydration`, `runtime-files-tool`, `sanitize-tool-result-for-model`, `turn-execution`, `runtime-media-request-parsing`, `runtime-video-generate-tool`, `native-tool-projection`, and `final-delivery-honesty` tests; workspace lint; API/web/runtime typecheck. `format:check` still reports an unrelated pre-existing `apps/web/app/app/_components/assistant-settings.test.tsx` formatting drift after runtime files were cleaned.

- **ADR-084 change-plan flow correction (`apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-07):** removed the mistaken embedded pricing picker from `Assistant Settings -> Payment settings` and restored the founder-approved single flow: `Payment settings -> Change plan -> standard /app/pricing -> pricing CTA -> review modal -> action`. `AssistantSettings` now calls the pricing-page redirect hook again instead of fetching/rendering a second pricing surface, `PricingPageView` keeps the management-aware review/confirm logic on the standard pricing CTA for FREE downgrade / cheaper-paid downgrade / upgrade even when billing truth must be loaded after degraded bootstrap, dead embedded-modal code and its technical locale copy were deleted, and focused web regressions now pin redirect behavior plus the no-browser-`confirm()` review flow. Verification: focused pricing/settings web tests, API typecheck, web typecheck. Repo-wide lint/format checks still report pre-existing unrelated runtime-file issues.

- **ADR-084 billing auth proxy matcher hotfix (`apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-07):** verified the still-live `Session expired` billing regression directly in `persai-dev` with Kubernetes logs and fixed the production-side auth seam. `api` logs showed successful billing-subscription reads followed by `enable-auto-renew` `401` requests with `userId=null`, while `web` logs simultaneously reported `Clerk: auth() was called but Clerk can't detect usage of clerkMiddleware()`. Root cause: the web API proxy route `app/api/v1/[[...path]]/route.ts` calls `auth()` to inject the session bearer, but `middleware.ts` excluded `/api/v1` from the Clerk matcher. The matcher now covers `/api/v1/*` too (`/(api|trpc)(.*)`), so Clerk middleware runs on the proxy route without changing the existing redirect-protect policy. Verification: focused web API proxy route test, web typecheck, and live `kubectl logs` inspection in `persai-dev`.

- **ADR-084 billing UX/auth follow-up (`apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-07):** closed the remaining product-flow gaps in the current billing UX slice without expanding backend scope. Signed-in pricing no longer relies on `bootstrap.plan` being present to decide whether a plan change is a managed downgrade/upgrade/free action: `PricingPageView` now refreshes billing subscription truth directly once Clerk is ready, keeps FREE downgrade / cheaper-paid downgrade / paid upgrade behind the review modal even when `/app/pricing` bootstraps in a degraded state, and blocks unsupported cross-period/cross-currency paid changes before submit instead of silently falling through to a raw mutation attempt. `Assistant Settings -> Payment settings` also no longer uses pricing-page redirect as the only change-plan entry: it now opens an in-product plan-picker overlay that reuses the same management-aware review flow so confirmations stay inside billing management. Verification: focused `pricing-page-view`, `assistant-settings`, and billing checkout page web tests plus `@persai/web` typecheck.

- **ADR-084 billing UX/auth deploy tails (`apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-07):** fixed the post-deploy billing UX regressions without expanding the recurring-management scope. Billing checkout, pricing, and payment-settings reads/actions now wait for Clerk auth rehydration and prefer a fresh token before declaring the session expired, which removes the fake reload-time `Session expired` state on active sessions. The remaining browser confirm on the billing surface was removed: `Assistant Settings -> Payment settings` now confirms disable-auto-renew inside the product modal, and pricing-card downgrade/free actions now use an internal billing review modal with explicit effect/date copy and post-action feedback instead of raw browser dialogs. Paid upgrades now also go through a product review modal that explains immediate activation plus billing-cycle reset before checkout starts. The founder-reported `Could not start checkout` upgrade symptom on reload was traced to the same auth-readiness gap plus overly generic pricing error mapping, so pricing now reports real session expiry honestly and no longer masks that path as a checkout-launch failure. Verification: focused `pricing-page-view`, `assistant-settings`, and billing checkout page web tests plus `@persai/web` typecheck.

- **ADR-084 recurring-management PROD cleanup (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-07):** hardened the remaining recurring-management logic seams without expanding scope. Cheaper paid downgrades no longer finalize through period-end cancellation fallback; PersAI now persists the cheaper-paid intent before any provider mutation, retries that same intent idempotently, and only switches to the target paid plan on the next trusted renewal path when the provider webhook amount/currency proves the cheaper cycle actually started. That closes the partial-failure gap where CloudPayments could already be downgraded while PersAI still thought the old plan was authoritative. Bind-only auto-renew enablement now lands as a dedicated `auto_renew_enabled` lifecycle transition so provider-managed recurring truth materializes even when there was nothing to "resume" and stale scheduled free/downgrade markers are cleared when auto-renew is really restored. Managed recurring upgrades keep their provider `subscriptions/update` mutation behind PersAI billing-event idempotency, and replay after partial failure now reuses the already-persisted provider subscription ref instead of reintroducing stale refs into PersAI truth. Pricing/UI state now only treats cheaper-paid scheduling as available when the server can really sustain that billing continuation, and cross-period/cross-currency paid changes now fail explicitly instead of being half-classified as a downgrade. Verification: focused `workspace-subscription-lifecycle`, `apply-workspace-subscription-billing-event`, `handle-cloudpayments-webhook`, `manage-assistant-billing-subscription`, `pricing-page-view`, and `assistant-settings` tests, plus workspace lint, format check, and API/web typecheck.

- **ADR-084 full recurring-management slice (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-07):** completed the missing product-grade recurring-management branches that were still outside the first recurring slice. Billing subscription state now exposes enable-auto-renew mode, scheduled downgrade/free preview, and richer recurring-management truth. The active billing API now has mode-aware `enable-auto-renew` and `change-plan` endpoints: paid/no-provider workspaces can start a bind-only checkout, canceled provider-managed subscriptions can resume in place, free/cheaper paid changes schedule at period end instead of falling into raw purchase checkout, and already-recurring paid upgrades use a full-payment managed upgrade contour without opening a second recurring contract. CloudPayments webhook handling now distinguishes payment-intent purpose so bind success only restores auto-renew while managed upgrades update the existing provider subscription anchor/amount for the new cycle. Pricing, checkout, and Assistant Settings were updated to use explicit confirm-step copy and purpose-aware checkout text, contracts were regenerated, and focused billing API/web regressions were refreshed. Verification: focused recurring-management API tests, focused pricing/settings/checkout web tests, contracts generation, API/web typecheck, workspace lint, and workspace format check.

- **Registration lifecycle init fix (`apps/api`, docs; subscription lifecycle 2026-05-07):** fixed the confirmed new-user gap where registration/onboarding could create `AppUser`, workspace membership, and even the assistant itself without ever entering PersAI subscription lifecycle truth. `CreateAssistantService` now initializes the workspace subscription from the active default registration policy the first time an assistant is created for a workspace that still has no `workspace_subscription`, so trial/default lifecycle truth no longer depends on a later admin/manual repair path. Added a focused `create-assistant.service` regression to lock that bootstrap. Verification: `corepack pnpm --filter @persai/api exec tsx test/create-assistant.service.test.ts`. Residual: `@persai/api` typecheck is currently blocked by a pre-existing unrelated repo issue (`apps/api/src/modules/workspace-management/application/manage-assistant-billing-subscription.service.ts` cannot resolve `@persai/contracts` in the current tree).

- **ADR-084 CloudPayments `culture` follow-up (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-07):** after re-reading the current CloudPayments `PaymentBlocks` recurrent docs, the checkout payload was tightened again: the constructor adapter now sends widget locale as documented `culture` instead of the previous `language` key. The web checkout page's local payload type/test fixtures were also synced to the real provider payload shape, including `recurrent` and `userInfo.accountId`, so future checkout debugging does not drift from the persisted intent contract. Verification: focused CloudPayments adapter test, focused checkout page test, API typecheck, and web typecheck.

- **ADR-084 billing UI polish + CloudPayments recurring follow-up (`apps/web`, `apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-07):** refined the paid billing UX after live founder review and another real recurring smoke. Highlighted pricing cards now keep the warmer premium fill only outside dark mode; in dark theme the recommended card stays on the normal dark surface and reads premium through a restrained gold border/top line instead of a painted panel. `Assistant Settings -> Limits & Plan` now keeps only one top-level CTA for paid plans (`Payment settings`), while `Change plan` lives inside the billing modal. Live recurring inspection for `alex@agse.ru` also confirmed that even the already-fixed top-level `recurrent` payload still produced `providerSubscriptionId=null`, so the CloudPayments constructor adapter now additionally sends the payer id through documented `userInfo.accountId` alongside the existing top-level `accountId`. Verification: focused pricing/settings web tests, focused CloudPayments adapter test, web typecheck, API typecheck, and live DB/log inspection of the latest paid intent/subscription.

- **ADR-084 CloudPayments recurring constructor payload fix (`apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-07):** corrected the provider checkout payload used for recurring-start payments after live evidence showed CloudPayments accepted the payment but returned no provider subscription id. The constructor adapter now sends `recurrent` as a top-level `initializationParams` field for `PaymentBlocks` instead of nesting it under provider-specific `data.CloudPayments`, and recurring-start checkouts always include a stable non-PII `accountId` by falling back to the workspace id when no provider customer ref exists yet. Focused adapter coverage now locks both the top-level recurrent shape and the account-id fallback. Verification: focused CloudPayments adapter test and `@persai/api` typecheck.

- **ADR-084 zero-price billing UI hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-07):** made zero-price plan truth explicit in the user plan visibility contract by exposing `effectivePlan.price`, so web billing surfaces can classify free access by `amount === 0` instead of lifecycle status or plan code. Pricing cards now render zero-price plans as `Free` / `Бесплатно`, active free access as `Indefinite` / `Бессрочно`, and keep free plans out of the billing-like `Payment settings` modal. Active paid plans now show `Payment settings` plus `Change plan` rather than a primary `Buy subscription` CTA, while current pricing cards use a subtle background instead of the premium gold border reserved for recommended plans. Verification: contracts generation, focused plan visibility/subscription lifecycle API tests, and focused pricing/settings/sidebar web tests.

- **ADR-084 CloudPayments constructor customization-arg fix (`apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** corrected a real integration drift against the current CloudPayments `PaymentBlocks` documentation. The checkout page had been flattening `appearance/components` into the first constructor argument together with initialization params, but the provider docs define customization as the second argument. The web checkout now passes `initializationParams` first and theme-aware `customizationParams` second again, with the checkout regression test updated to lock that API shape. This should restore provider-supported form theming paths, including the background/appearance behavior that depended on CloudPayments honoring customization keys from the right argument slot. Verification: focused billing checkout page test, workspace lint, format check, API typecheck, and web typecheck.

- **Pricing card premium-border polish (`apps/web`, docs; product UI 2026-05-06):** softened the pricing-page emphasis so the current plan and the recommended highlighted plan no longer look like the same heavy filled card. Both emphasized cards now keep the shared base card background, while the emphasis moves to a thin restrained gold gradient border plus a matching top highlight line that reads as premium in both light and dark themes without adding noisy glow. Verification: focused `pricing-page-view` web test, workspace lint, format check, API typecheck, and web typecheck.

- **ADR-084 paid billing settings follow-through (`apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** fixed the live product gap where successful paid access without a provider-managed recurring contract could briefly show `Payment settings` and then replace it with `Change plan`. `Assistant Settings -> Limits & Plan` now treats paid access itself as the entry condition for billing settings, so one-time paid subscriptions keep a stable `Payment settings` entry while recurring-only controls remain conditional. When billing state is paid, the top CTA pair is now `Buy subscription` plus `Payment settings`; the modal shows truthful access-period details for one-time paid subscriptions, hides unavailable recurring actions instead of rendering dead disabled controls, and uses dedicated non-recurring copy instead of implying auto-renew management exists. Verification: focused `assistant-settings` web test, workspace lint, format check, API typecheck, and web typecheck.

- **ADR-084 recurring billing PROD hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** closed the remaining code-side P0 recurring billing seams before live rollout. The authenticated recurring-management endpoints are now explicitly covered by `ClerkAuthMiddleware`, with identity-access regression coverage so `Payment settings` cannot silently lose `resolvedAppUser` again. CloudPayments webhook billing-event idempotency now uses a SHA-256 digest of the raw provider payload instead of placeholder-based refs, removing the earlier collision risk when provider ids were missing, and payment-intent reconciliation now resolves deterministically instead of taking an undefined `findFirst` winner from a broad `OR`. Recurring-management reads also now surface failed `cancel_api` sync attempts from durable billing events so the UI does not falsely claim auto-renew is still on when provider cancellation already succeeded but PersAI lifecycle apply failed. The public webhook controller now preserves underlying HTTP status classes (`403` / `404` / `500`) instead of flattening every failure to `400 webhook_failed`. Verification: focused recurring adapter/webhook/subscription-management/payment-intent/auth/web tests, then workspace lint, format check, API typecheck, and web typecheck.

- **ADR-084 recurring billing honesty hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** closed the remaining post-audit truth/UX tails as one coherent recurring-billing hardening pass. Embedded checkout no longer returns to chat as `pending` when the user simply leaves the in-form CloudPayments surface; `pending` is now reserved for real provider-backed confirmation flow, while abandonment degrades to the existing calm retry path. Billing management now scopes `paymentMethodLabel` to provider events that belong to the active recurring contract instead of any recent workspace event, and `Payment settings` no longer fabricates fallback facts like `Free`, `Off`, or a generic payment method when refresh fails; the entry stays reachable, but billing details are shown as unknown until the next successful sync. `Limits & Plan` summary copy also now uses `Access until` for `canceled` subscription-ending state instead of the misleading `Next billing`. Verification: focused billing-subscription-management API test, focused checkout/settings web tests, workspace lint, format check, API typecheck, and web typecheck.

- **ADR-084 recurring webhook stale-subscription hardening (`apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** closed the remaining high-severity provider-event corruption seam in recurring billing. CloudPayments webhook resolution no longer falls back from an unmatched `SubscriptionId` onto the current subscription via `AccountId` / `providerCustomerRef`, so stale or out-of-order recurring provider events cannot silently mutate the wrong recurring contract. `ApplyWorkspaceSubscriptionBillingEventService` now also ignores provider-origin lifecycle events whose provider customer/subscription refs conflict with the currently linked subscription, adding a second guard against old-provider-event drift. Focused webhook/apply regressions were added, ADR-083 wording now reflects that global lifecycle settings already live in `Admin > Billing Settings`, and ADR-084 progress wording now treats the concrete provider-adapter slice as completed while keeping live validation/evidence-driven hardening as the active follow-through. Verification: focused CloudPayments webhook and billing-event-apply tests, workspace lint, format check, API typecheck, and web typecheck.

- **ADR-084 PROD recurring hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** closed the main audit tails before live recurring rollout. Provider-managed recurring upgrades no longer open a second recurring-start checkout on top of an existing provider subscription; PersAI now fails that path fast until a real in-place subscription-update slice exists. Fallback/cancel/reversal expiry paths now clear provider subscription refs before landing in `expired_fallback`, billing-management state now only treats active paid recurring rows as provider-managed truth and preserves the latest known payment method label after auto-renew disablement, the web recurring-management CTA stays reachable even when the refresh request fails, checkout help copy distinguishes recurring-start from honest one-time fallback, and the web recurring-management client now uses generated contracts instead of handwritten fetch calls. Assistant-facing subscription knowledge now also resolves through the effective subscription-state service instead of raw row/default fallback reads, which keeps narrative subscription context aligned with real lifecycle truth. OpenAPI now documents the real `400` path for malformed payment-intent ids, runtime coverage includes the one-time fallback checkout projection, and contracts were regenerated. Verification: contracts generation, focused payment-intent/lifecycle/runtime quota/web checkout/settings tests, then full workspace lint/format and API/web/runtime/contracts typecheck gates.

- **ADR-084 PROD recurring billing (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** completed the first production recurring subscription slice on top of PersAI-owned payment intents and ADR-083 lifecycle truth. CloudPayments constructor checkout can now open a real recurring-start contour for supported methods (`card` now; unsupported methods degrade honestly to one-shot), payment intents persist recurring capability state, trusted webhook handling now projects `recurrent`, renewal failure/recovery, and provider cancel events into billing lifecycle truth, and paid cancellation is represented honestly through `cancelAtPeriodEnd` plus period-end fallback. The product UI now exposes recurring management in `Assistant Settings -> Limits & Plan`: paid recurring users see `Payment settings` instead of `Change plan`, can inspect server-truth auto-renew/next-charge/payment-method state, disable auto-renew through PersAI API, and follow the provider-managed payment-method update path. `quota_status` checkout payloads now also tell the runtime whether the selected method opens a recurring checkout or a one-shot fallback, so the assistant can describe billing contours honestly from one place. Verification: focused API recurring tests (payment intents, CloudPayments adapter/webhooks, billing event application, lifecycle fallback, billing subscription management), focused runtime quota-status test, focused web assistant-settings test, then full workspace lint/format plus API/web/runtime typecheck.

- **ADR-084 checkout theme + quota price-label hardening (`apps/web`, `apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** fixed two still-live billing UX issues after another founder validation pass. First, the embedded CloudPayments checkout page now sends PersAI theme overrides as real `PaymentBlocks` `options` instead of the constructor's secondary configuration slot, which is a better match for the provider docs/bundle shape and should finally address the remaining dark-theme white inner surface / receipt-row mismatch. Second, `quota_status` no longer leaves tariff rendering to raw `amountMinor` interpretation: internal quota reads and the shared runtime contract now expose `visiblePlans[].amountMajor` plus localized `priceLabel`, and the tool guidance explicitly tells the model to quote prices from those major/display fields so `98000` does not leak into tariff comparisons. Live verification also confirmed that at least one recent payment now truly activates on the PersAI side (`workspace_payment_intent 82315353...` -> `succeeded`, `payment_activated` applied, subscription `basic active`), even though the founder user's separate recent intents remain `checkout_ready`. Verification: focused checkout page, quota-status API/runtime, and turn-execution tests plus API/web/runtime typecheck.

- **ADR-084 checkout PaymentBlocks init hotfix (`apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** corrected the live `persai-dev` checkout regression where the billing screen still degraded to `Оплата сейчас недоступна / Connection error` even while the payment-intent API request itself returned `200`. Live API logs during the failing founder retry proved the intent read succeeded, so the failure had moved to the next client-side stage: `CloudPayments PaymentBlocks` initialization. The recent checkout shell fix had added an immediate `blocksApp.update(...)` call right after `mount()`, but the page already remounts the constructor whenever the resolved theme changes, so that extra update step was redundant and could abort the provider init path. The hotfix removes the post-mount `update()` call and keeps theme-aware customization only in the constructor payload. Verification: focused billing checkout page test and `@persai/web` typecheck.

- **ADR-084 checkout localhost API-base hotfix (`packages/contracts`, `infra/helm`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** fixed the live `persai-dev` regression where the billing checkout screen could degrade to `Оплата сейчас недоступна / Connection error` even though the payment-intent API itself was healthy. Root cause: the generated contracts fetch mutator still trusted `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3001/api/v1` in the browser, while other web paths already ignored localhost and fell back to same-origin `/api/v1`; the live `web` deployment also still carried that bad public env value. `packages/contracts/src/mutator/custom-fetch.ts` now applies the same browser-safe localhost guard as the main assistant API client, and the dev Helm values now explicitly set `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=/api/v1` for `web` so the deployed bundle/proxy path stays browser-safe. Verification: contracts typecheck, web typecheck, `helm template persai-dev infra/helm -f infra/helm/values-dev.yaml`.

- **ADR-084 live `check` UUID lookup hotfix (`apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** fixed the live CloudPayments `check` webhook crash where short provider refs like `InvoiceId` were being sent through the Prisma UUID `workspace_payment_intents.id` lookup together with ordinary provider references. `HandleCloudpaymentsWebhookService.resolvePaymentIntent()` now routes only UUID-shaped candidates through the `id` clause while keeping all candidates available for `providerSessionRef` / `providerPaymentRef`, so the webhook matcher no longer throws `Error creating UUID, invalid length` before intent resolution. Added a focused regression test that locks the short-ref path. Verification: focused CloudPayments webhook test and API typecheck.

- **ADR-084 checkout theme parity fix (`apps/web`, `apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** replaced the previous single forced CloudPayments embedded palette with explicit light/dark theme-aware checkout presets so the provider form no longer appears visually inverted between PersAI dark and light themes. The checkout shell now tracks the resolved app theme and passes matching `PaymentBlocks` colors for form background, text, labels, and the receipt row, while a focused regression test locks the light-theme payload shape. Live `persai-dev` inspection after the founder's latest retries still shows recent payment intents stuck in `checkout_ready` with no billing events, so this slice fixes the checkout visual regression but does not yet resolve the upstream provider callback gap. Verification: focused billing checkout page test, API typecheck, web typecheck, workspace lint, and workspace format check.

- **ADR-084 Android billing return app-link fix (`apps/web`, mobile shell docs; billing/mobile continuity 2026-05-06):** added an Android App Links bridge for the Capacitor shell so verified `https://persai.dev/app/*` URLs can reopen `com.persai.app` after external bank-app hops instead of falling back to the browser. `apps/web` now serves `.well-known/assetlinks.json` for the current shipped Android signing fingerprint, and the web app mounts a Capacitor `appUrlOpen` bridge that soft-routes incoming verified URLs back into the matching Next.js in-app path. The Android shell manifest now claims only `/app/*` so landing/marketing routes stay browser-owned. iOS universal links remain explicitly out of scope until Apple signing / associated-domains truth exists. Verification: `@persai/web` typecheck plus static asset / manifest inspection.

- **ADR-079 retrieved-knowledge developer-tail hotfix (`apps/runtime`, docs; live skill grounding 2026-05-06):** moved the per-turn `Retrieved Knowledge Context` block out of synthetic assistant history injection and into `developerInstructions`, alongside the existing server-owned routing/open-media guidance. This removes dependence on the assistant-message injection contour for runtime-owned grounding, makes the bounded retrieval block visible in provider developer traces, and keeps durable history hydration separate from runtime-owned retrieval evidence. Verification: focused `@persai/runtime` `turn-execution.service.test.ts`, runtime typecheck.

- **ADR-084 live webhook HMAC hotfix (`apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** investigated the live `persai-dev` payment failure after CloudPayments notifications were enabled and confirmed that `Check` webhooks were reaching the API but being rejected with `CloudPayments webhook signature verification failed`. The trusted webhook handler now prefers `Content-HMAC` before falling back to `X-Content-HMAC`, which matches the provider's POST notification behavior more safely for the encoded raw body while preserving the existing HMAC verification gate. Added a regression test that covers the mixed-header case where `X-Content-HMAC` is wrong but `Content-HMAC` is valid. Verification: focused CloudPayments webhook test, API typecheck, workspace lint, format check, runtime/web typecheck, and `helm template persai-dev infra/helm -f infra/helm/values-dev.yaml`.

- **ADR-084 quota tool one-path cleanup (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, `packages/config`, `infra/helm`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** made `quota_status` the single assistant-facing surface for live plan facts, quota usage, plan comparison, and guarded checkout-link creation. The runtime checkout path no longer regex-matches raw user text for billing phrases; it now uses the intentional tool action (`confirmed=true`) without lexical hard-codes. Internal quota reads now return richer visible-plan facts (description, enabled tools, localized presentation copy, highlight bullets, and key quota limits), and quota checkout now returns absolute `checkoutPageUrl` plus `checkoutSignInUrl` when `PERSAI_WEB_BASE_URL` is configured, alongside the canonical in-app checkout path. The active `persai-dev` GitOps values now set `PERSAI_WEB_BASE_URL=https://persai.dev` for `api` and `runtime`, while the shared chart defaults keep the value empty so other environments remain explicit. Prompt/template defaults and active ADR/API/test-plan docs were updated so plan facts stay on `quota_status` instead of bouncing to duplicated subscription knowledge guidance. Verification: focused quota-status API/runtime tests, API/runtime/web typecheck, workspace lint, format check, and `helm template persai-dev infra/helm -f infra/helm/values-dev.yaml`.

- **ADR-084 embedded checkout PROD hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `apps/runtime`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-06):** closed the highest-risk seams found in the independent audit of the new embedded checkout contour. CloudPayments constructor payloads no longer write PersAI `userId` into provider-facing `accountId`, webhook/lifecycle paths no longer overwrite `providerCustomerRef` from incoming `AccountId`, and recurring is now explicitly disabled in the active production contour until trusted recurrent lifecycle support exists. The runtime/internal quota checkout seam now fails instead of reporting `checkout_created` when payment-intent checkout creation failed, and the web checkout/chat return flow is now truth-safer too: expired/terminal intents do not remount a live form, legacy widget-shaped persisted rows render a calm retry shell, embedded fail exits return `failed`, embedded success returns as optimistic `pending`, and chat billing banners reconcile by `paymentIntentId` until authoritative server truth settles. Verification: focused constructor-adapter, CloudPayments webhook, internal runtime quota checkout, internal runtime quota status, runtime quota tool, checkout page, and chat page tests plus workspace lint/format and contracts/api/web/runtime typecheck.

- **ADR-086 web composer chip alignment (`apps/web`, docs; async media jobs UI 2026-05-06):** moved the web `activeMediaJobs` chip rail in `ChatInput` to a right-anchored overlay above the composer, so pending media chips stay near the send side instead of stretching from the left and visually covering attached-file previews or the left edge of the input. Verification: focused `chat-input` component test.

- **ADR-086 web open-media pass-through hotfix (`apps/api`, docs; async media jobs 2026-05-06):** fixed the concrete live web seam that was still preventing the new runtime `## Open Media Jobs` developer block from appearing. `SendWebChatTurnService` and `StreamWebChatTurnService` were already reading durable `openMediaJobs` for follow-up turns, but their local `buildNative*TurnInput()` helpers dropped that field before the request reached the native runtime adapter. Both helpers now forward `openMediaJobs` all the way into the runtime payload, and the existing sync/stream web routing regressions now explicitly assert that the forwarded native input contains the open-job list. Verification: focused `send-web-chat-turn` and `stream-web-chat-turn` tests plus API typecheck.

- **ADR-084 embedded constructor checkout + billing amount invariant (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/contracts`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-05):** replaced the old CloudPayments popup widget contour with a persisted constructor-based embedded checkout flow. Payment intents now normalize on `checkout.mode=embedded`, the provider adapter stores a `persai.billing.cloudpaymentsConstructorCheckout.v1` payload, and the web checkout page mounts `paymentblocks.js` inside a minimal premium payment sheet instead of launching `cloudpayments.js` as a popup. The same slice fixed the money invariant across pricing/payment intents/provider payloads so presentation amounts stay in major units but checkout/provider logic uses explicit minor-unit conversion, closing the `980 -> 9.8` bug. Recurring is intentionally disabled in the active production contour until trusted webhook/lifecycle support is ready, so checkout no longer implies provider-managed renewals are already wired. Verification: contracts generation, Prisma generate, focused constructor-adapter test, focused payment-intent service test, focused checkout page test, workspace lint, workspace format check, contracts/api/web/runtime typecheck.

- **CI runtime test harness hardening (`apps/runtime`, docs; test reliability 2026-05-05):** fixed the failing post-push GitHub Actions `CI > Test` job by removing the brittle shared-process runtime package test entrypoint as the authoritative gate. `@persai/runtime` package `test` now runs a new isolated harness (`test/run-suite-isolated.ts` + `test/run-one.ts`) that executes the same exported runtime test modules in separate `tsx` child processes, so order-sensitive state from earlier modules can no longer poison later ones. The follow-up runtime regression fix also made `turn-execution.service.test.ts` honest under isolated execution by defaulting synthetic turn requests to the real warmed bundle hash and resetting the provider mock reply before the open-media-jobs case. Verification: focused isolated `turn-execution` run, `@persai/runtime` package test, full workspace `pnpm run test`, runtime lint, and workspace `format:check`.

- **ADR-086 open-media context developer-tail fix (`apps/runtime`, docs; async media jobs 2026-05-05):** moved `openMediaJobs` prompt truth out of hydrated assistant-history messages and into `developerInstructions`, next to the other server-owned per-turn guidance. `TurnContextHydrationService` no longer injects `# Open Async Media Jobs` as a faux assistant message, while `TurnExecutionService` now renders a dedicated `## Open Media Jobs` developer section with durable server truth (`kind`, `status`, `createdAt`, `startedAt`) and explicit instructions to answer progress from that truth instead of inventing queue state or silently re-running media tools. Verification: focused `turn-context-hydration` and `turn-execution` runtime tests.

- **ADR-079 skill-state split cleanup (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-bundle`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; routing reliability 2026-05-05):** repaired the broken Skill activation/deactivation path end to end by separating persisted `skillDecisionState` from `skillCadenceState`, introducing a dedicated runtime `SkillStateRoutingService` plus new admin preset `skill_state_classifier`, and migrating chat persistence off the old single `auto_skill_routing_state` cell. Background Skill checks now persist through a compact `activate | deactivate | no_change` contract, queued checks no longer block later active/inactive writes, failed checks clear their queued flag for retry instead of wedging, new chats bootstrap honestly at message `3`, and older chats with newly enabled Skills force a check on the next user message. The remaining ordinary-router cleanup is now finished too: the ordinary router prompt/schema/path no longer chooses Skill ids, ordinary turns may only invoke the dedicated skill-state classifier for bounded foreground activation, and deactivation stays background/forced-check only. Follow-up audit cleanup also tightened `sticky_skill_reuse` to same-topic/high-signal follow-ups, hardened the equal-index persistence guard against stale `active` resurrection, aligned `turnRouting` transport shape (`ordinarySourcePriorityMode`, `skillState`) across runtime/API/web surfaces, moved `skillRetrievalState` onto the shared `AssistantChat` domain/repository boundary, and extended `AssistantWebChatState` contracts/generated models so the split persisted Skill state is no longer dropped from the web-chat surface. Both ordinary and skill-state classifier calls now use `1200` output caps with one invalid-JSON retry and raw-output warning logs. Verification: workspace lint, API/runtime/web typecheck, focused runtime `turn-routing` / `turn-execution`, focused API `auto-skill-routing-state`, `manage-assistant-skills`, `manage-admin-skills`, `send-web-chat-turn`, and `stream-web-chat-turn` tests; `format:check` still reports a pre-existing unrelated drift in `apps/web/app/app/_components/chat-input.tsx`.

- **ADR-086 deferred media acknowledgement hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `apps/web`, docs; async media jobs 2026-05-05):** fixed the last misleading first-reply seam after async media acceptance. The media tool `Use code default` prompt text on [`/admin/presets`](https://persai.dev/admin/presets) now explicitly tells the model that `action="deferred"` means the request was only accepted into the async lane and the final image/video is not finished in the current turn. `TurnExecutionService` now also hard-normalizes any deferred generated-media turn with zero inline artifacts to one standard honest acknowledgement (`doing / will send separately`) before that text reaches web or Telegram, so channel differences and model drift can no longer produce “already made / here is the result” replies for unfinished async media jobs. Verification: focused `seed-tool-catalog`, `deferred-media-acknowledgement`, `runtime-media-request-parsing`, and `use-chat` tests plus API/runtime typecheck.

- **ADR-086 single-path cleanup and hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `apps/web`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; async media jobs 2026-05-05):** removed the last transitional acceptance/read seams so generated media now uses one production contour end to end. Sync web, stream web, and Telegram turns no longer run the old detector-only `assistant-web-async-media-lane` shortcut before the model decides to call a tool; durable media jobs are now accepted only from the real tool-boundary deferral path, worker execution accepts only persisted `directToolExecution` payloads, and the duplicate web `activeMediaJobs` read layer has been collapsed into `AssistantMediaJobService`. The web chat cache also now updates `activeMediaJobs` synchronously during thread snapshot writes, which closes a switch-back/reconnect drift bug and keeps pending chips tied to durable server truth until delivery/failure clears them. Verification: workspace lint, format check, API/web typecheck, and focused `send-web-chat-turn`, `stream-web-chat-turn`, `handle-internal-telegram-turn`, `assistant-media-job-scheduler`, `assistant-media-job-completion-delivery`, `manage-web-chat-list`, `runtime-media-job-run`, `use-chat`, and `chat-input` tests.

- **ADR-086 web pending-media continuity UX (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; async media jobs 2026-05-05):** web chat now exposes the active media-job operation in durable `activeMediaJobs` truth and surfaces that state directly above the composer as quiet horizontal pending chips like `Генерирую фото 1:42` / `Генерирую видео 0:38`, with compact aggregation for larger queues. Sync and stream web turn responses now also return the refreshed open-job projection immediately after acknowledgement persistence, so the indicator appears without `F5`, survives chat switch/reconnect from server truth, and polls the authoritative chat history until delivery/failure clears it. Verification: API/web typecheck, focused `send-web-chat-turn`, `stream-web-chat-turn`, and `chat-input` tests, workspace lint, and format check.

- **ADR-086 open-media continuity context (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; async media jobs 2026-05-05):** later ordinary turns no longer have to guess whether generated media work is already in progress. Sync web, stream web, and Telegram ordinary turns now pass durable open media-job summaries into the runtime turn request, and `TurnContextHydrationService` injects a bounded prompt block describing queued/running background media work from server truth. This makes post-acceptance conversation honesty much safer while leaving backend job/delivery ownership unchanged. Verification: runtime/API/web typecheck, focused `turn-context-hydration`, `send-native-web-chat-turn`, `stream-native-web-chat-turn`, and `send-native-telegram-turn` tests, workspace lint, and format check.

- **ADR-086 tool-boundary media deferral cutover (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; async media jobs 2026-05-05):** ordinary native turns no longer have to finish generated image/video work inline before responding. When the model actually calls `image_generate`, `image_edit`, or `video_generate`, the runtime tool path now defers that work into a durable `assistant_media_jobs` row through a new internal enqueue seam, returns a structured deferred tool result so the model can write the immediate acknowledgement in the same turn, and lets backend completion delivery own the final media send. Web sync/stream and Telegram paths now also bind the persisted acknowledgement assistant message back onto those already-enqueued jobs, so streamed acknowledgement text is no longer ahead of durable job truth. Verification: runtime/API/web typecheck, focused `turn-execution`, `send-web-chat-turn`, `stream-web-chat-turn`, and `handle-internal-telegram-turn` tests, workspace lint, and format check.

- **ADR-086 direct media worker execution foundation (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; async media jobs 2026-05-05):** the async media worker seam no longer assumes every background media job must be re-executed through a second synthetic LLM tool run. Durable media-job payloads can now carry a normalized direct tool execution request for `image_generate`, `image_edit`, or `video_generate`, and `RuntimeMediaJobRunService` can execute those persisted tool requests directly through the existing native media tool services. This keeps the already-landed worker lane compatible with the older prompt-based media-job payload while preparing the correct cutover path where ordinary turns persist the actual media tool call and background execution does not burn a second round of tokens just to rediscover the same action.
