Verification: focused `assistant-media-job-scheduler` and `runtime-media-job-run` tests, API/runtime typecheck, and workspace format check.

- **ADR-086 Slice 4 completion-turn seam (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; async media jobs 2026-05-05):** finished async media jobs now pass through a dedicated bounded completion-framing seam before final delivery. The API side now reads the latest canonical chat history, current assistant runtime bundle, and worker result/artifact summary, then calls a new internal runtime endpoint for idempotent fresh-history framing keyed by the media job instead of reusing the old request-time media contour. `AssistantMediaJobCompletionDeliveryService` now applies that framing before final web/Telegram delivery honesty correction, while job state, artifacts, quota settlement, and delivery truth remain backend-owned. Verification: focused `assistant-media-job-completion-delivery`, `runtime-media-job-run`, and new `runtime-media-job-completion` tests plus runtime-contract/API/runtime typecheck, workspace lint, and format check.

- **ADR-086 Telegram cutover onto the shared async media lane (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; async media jobs 2026-05-05):** ordinary accepted Telegram generated image/audio/video requests no longer depend on the old synchronous request-time final-media contour. `HandleInternalTelegramTurnService` now routes clear Telegram generated-media asks through the same durable async acceptance/enqueue path already used for web, so the webhook can respond quickly with an honest acknowledgement while `assistant_media_jobs` owns the long-running work. The shared completion-delivery service is now surface-aware and can deliver `completion_pending` jobs back into Telegram asynchronously, while the runtime media-job seam now carries the real job surface instead of hardcoding `web`. The dedicated fresh-history completion-turn seam is still pending, and the detector remains conservative, so some ambiguous media asks may still miss the async lane until the final cleanup slice. Verification: focused `assistant-web-async-media-lane`, `handle-internal-telegram-turn`, `assistant-media-job-completion-delivery`, `assistant-media-job-scheduler`, `telegram-webhook-proxy`, `send-native-telegram-turn`, and `runtime-media-job-run` tests plus runtime-contract/API/runtime typecheck.

- **ADR-086 Slice 3 web async enqueue and completion delivery (`apps/api`, docs; async media jobs 2026-05-05):** wired real web chat enqueue into the durable `assistant_media_jobs` lane and removed the `completion_pending` dead-end for web delivery. Clear generated image/audio/video requests on the web sync/stream turn paths now get an immediate acknowledgement assistant message, create a durable media job, and return without waiting for final provider completion; the backend scheduler now also consumes `completion_pending` rows and delivers finished artifacts into the originating web chat, persisting `completionAssistantMessageId` and terminal `delivered` / `failed` state. This still leaves Telegram on the old synchronous media contour and still keeps a conservative classifier, so some ambiguous web media asks may continue to use the legacy sync path until later ADR-086 cleanup. Verification: focused `assistant-web-async-media-lane`, `assistant-media-job-completion-delivery`, and `assistant-media-job-scheduler` tests plus API/web typecheck, workspace lint, and format check.

- **ADR-086 Slice 3 foundation (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; async media jobs 2026-05-05):** landed the first real worker/execution seam behind the durable `assistant_media_jobs` model. The API side now has a backend-owned media-job scheduler with durable claim TTL, retry timing, persisted request/result payloads, and `completion_pending` progression. The runtime side now exposes `POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/media-jobs/run`, which executes a synthetic tool-enabled media job via `createBackgroundTaskToolRun` instead of reviving an old user turn inside the live chat thread. This still does **not** cut ordinary web/Telegram media requests over to the async lane yet, but it removes the need to fake execution later with ad-hoc background logic. Verification: focused `assistant-media-job-scheduler` and `runtime-media-job-run` tests plus runtime-contract/API/runtime/web typecheck.

- **Billing UI + chat navigation hardening (`apps/web`, docs; product UI 2026-05-05):** removed the extra top chrome from the pricing and checkout surfaces and fixed broken sidebar chat switching. Public/in-app pricing no longer renders the top `Назад` plus `Вы в аккаунте` row, the current `/app/billing/checkout/:paymentIntentId` page no longer renders its top back button, and sidebar chat/new-chat navigation now uses real `router.push(...)` transitions instead of a raw `window.history.pushState(...)` shortcut that could change only the URL while leaving stale page content mounted. Verification: focused `pricing-page-view`, billing checkout page, and sidebar web tests plus workspace lint, format check, and API/web typecheck.

- **ADR-086 Slice 2 foundation (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; async media jobs 2026-05-05):** landed the first durable implementation slice for generated `image` / `audio` / `video` async orchestration without yet switching execution onto the new lane. Added Prisma schema + migration for `assistant_media_jobs` with explicit generated-media kind/status enums, added a read-only API service for open media jobs, and extended web continuity responses/contracts so chat list rows and chat-history reads can now expose optional `activeMediaJobs` alongside `activeTurn`. This keeps current text/media execution behavior intact while establishing durable job truth and the restore contract required for chat switch / reconnect / `F5` continuity in later slices. Verification: focused `manage-web-chat-list.service` and `web-chat-media-job.service` tests plus contracts/API/web typecheck.

- **Telegram media-delivery honesty hardening (`apps/api`, docs; Telegram reliability 2026-05-05):** fixed the live Telegram bug where a runtime/media turn could still behave as if media had already been delivered even when the shared delivery seam returned zero successful Telegram attachments. Live GKE evidence for assistant `8b27ed67-b9dc-4c9e-b1dc-8b09852eaaee` showed repeated long-running `/telegram-webhook/:assistantId` requests plus duplicate updates during image-heavy turns, but the concrete false-success bug was narrower: `TelegramChannelAdapterService` treated `turnResult.media.length > 0` as delivery success even when `MediaDeliveryService.deliver()` returned `attachments=[]`. Telegram outbound replies now base `mediaAlreadyDelivered` on the real delivered attachment count, and when `0/N` artifacts are delivered the final assistant text is passed through the existing delivery-honesty correction before being sent, so Telegram no longer claims a photo/file was sent when nothing actually reached the chat. Verification: focused `telegram-webhook-proxy.controller`, `telegram-bot.client`, `final-delivery-honesty`, and `handle-internal-telegram-turn` tests, plus workspace lint, API/web typecheck, and targeted format check.

- **ADR-084 checkout truth hardening (`apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-05):** fixed a live production regression where opening checkout for certain legacy users silently created a fresh `workspace_subscription` from the default registration plan, which in turn emitted `trial_started`, reset quota-period dates, and made limits look “new” before any payment succeeded. `ResolveEffectiveSubscriptionStateService` now exposes a read-only billing-state path for checkout/payment-intent decisions, and `ManageAssistantPaymentIntentsService` uses that read-only path instead of the write-through initializer. Verification: focused `subscription-state-resolve` and `manage-assistant-payment-intents` API tests, workspace lint, format check, and API/web typecheck.

- **ADR-084 live hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-05):** fixed two production billing regressions found during live validation after the widget-first slice. The CloudPayments checkout page now reads `paymentIntentId` from real route params via `next/navigation` instead of relying on a client-page `params` prop shape, which stopped live requests from hitting `GET /assistant/billing/payment-intents/undefined`. The payment-intent read path now rejects malformed ids with an explicit `400` instead of a Prisma UUID `500`. Manual/admin paid activation now clears stale `trialStartedAt/trialEndsAt` when an existing trial subscription transitions to active paid, and `Admin > Ops` now chooses the user-directory `Next date` by lifecycle status so active paid rows prefer `currentPeriodEndsAt` instead of leaking an old trial end date. Verification: focused payment-intent and lifecycle API tests, focused checkout/admin-ops web tests, workspace lint, format check, and API/web typecheck. Live GKE logs during the fix confirmed the pre-fix failure shape as repeated `/assistant/billing/payment-intents/undefined` requests.

- **ADR-084 widget-first audit cleanup (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-05):** ran a repo-wide ADR-084 cleanup pass after the CloudPayments widget-first slice and removed several leftover legacy seams. Deleted the dead provider-pull sync path (`SyncWorkspaceSubscriptionService`) and the orphaned `NullBillingProviderAdapter`, updated the billing provider port/runtime tool copy to stop describing pre-Slice-9 behavior, stopped the widget adapter from silently constraining payment methods based on the old `paymentMethodClass` branch, cleaned the checkout/chat copy so it no longer talks about “future webhook slices”, humanized the chat billing banner plan label, and stripped one-shot billing return query params from `/app/chat` after they are consumed. Verification: focused CloudPayments adapter test, runtime quota/turn-execution tests, billing checkout page test, API/web typecheck. `app/app/chat/page.test.tsx` could not complete in this environment because `vitest` ran out of memory even after retrying with a larger heap, so chat-page behavior was validated via typecheck plus changed-file review instead. `format:check` still reports existing repo-wide/generated Prettier drift.

- **ADR-084 Slice 9 CloudPayments widget-first wiring (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-05):** landed the first real provider adapter without creating a second billing truth surface. `POST /api/v1/assistant/billing/payment-intents` now uses a concrete CloudPayments widget adapter that reads the encrypted API Secret plus widget public terminal id from `Admin > Tools`, returns a persisted `checkout.mode=widget` payload, and keeps payment-intent state PersAI-owned before any provider UI opens. `/app/billing/checkout/:paymentIntentId` now launches `cloudpayments.js` from that persisted payload and returns to chat with the same `success` / `failed` / `pending` envelope while still waiting for trusted webhook confirmation. CloudPayments webhook parsing now accepts widget-style `externalId` and `metadata/data` so widget-originated payments reconcile back to the correct intent before ADR-083 lifecycle activation. Verification: focused billing credential, CloudPayments widget adapter, CloudPayments webhook, and checkout-page tests plus contracts/API/web typecheck. Live provider smoke with real credentials is still pending.

- **Telegram completed-turn safety hardening (`apps/api`, docs; delivery reliability 2026-05-05):** hardened the synchronous Telegram turn path so a successfully completed runtime turn is no longer discarded just because the post-runtime tail fails. `HandleInternalTelegramTurnService` now treats three post-runtime stages differently: if assistant-message persistence fails after runtime completion, it still returns the completed text reply to Telegram instead of falling into the generic temporary-unavailable path; if quota accounting fails, the user reply still goes out and the failure is logged as non-blocking; if Telegram update finalization fails, the turn still completes and the claim is best-effort released to avoid stale in-flight metadata. Focused regression coverage now locks these cases, including the safe degradation where runtime media is dropped only when no assistant message row exists to anchor delivery. Verification: focused `handle-internal-telegram-turn` test, workspace lint, API typecheck, and web typecheck. `format:check` still reports the existing repo-wide unrelated/generated Prettier drift.

- **ADR-084 Slice 8 assistant quota-tool checkout (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-05):** completed the user-requested assistant billing slice without creating a second billing tool. The existing `quota_status` tool now returns current/public plan context alongside quota state so the assistant can explain upgrades from the same surface, and it can create guarded checkout only after explicit confirmation. The new runtime-to-API path creates a normal PersAI payment intent and returns the existing `/app/billing/checkout/:paymentIntentId` entry path, so subscription activation still depends on trusted billing lifecycle truth rather than assistant text, client return params, or tester override state. Verification: focused `read-internal-runtime-quota-status`, `create-internal-runtime-quota-checkout`, `runtime-quota-status-tool`, and `turn-execution` tests; workspace lint; API/web/runtime typecheck. `format:check` still reports pre-existing repo-wide Prettier drift in unrelated/generated files.

- **ADR-084 pre-Slice-8 billing hardening (`apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-04):** closed two billing-base risks before the assistant billing tool slice. `ApplyWorkspaceSubscriptionBillingEventService` no longer fails trusted first paid activation just because `workspace_subscription` has not been initialized by an earlier read path; it now records the billing event first and lets the lifecycle paid-activation path create/link the subscription row when needed. `ManageAssistantPaymentIntentsService` now resolves user billing truth from the real workspace subscription / default-registration initialization path and explicitly ignores tester `assistantPlanOverrideCode` plus quota fallback state, so `Admin > Ops > Plan Control` cannot leak into user checkout decisions. Verification: focused `apply-workspace-subscription-billing-event` and `manage-assistant-payment-intents` API tests, then workspace lint/format/typecheck gates.

- **ADR-084 Slice 7 admin manual payment and Ops support (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-04):** replaced the old fallback-only implicit paid restore with an explicit manual/admin paid activation path in `Admin > Ops`. Billing support actions now accept a selected paid plan plus billing period, write the result through PersAI lifecycle truth as `source=admin` instead of pretending a provider invoice/session exists, and expose the latest paid-activation source back in Ops so support can clearly distinguish manual/admin payment from provider-driven billing. Also reconciled the contract drift where `initialize_lifecycle_now` already existed in code/UI but was missing from the OpenAPI billing-support action enum. Verification: focused `manage-admin-ops-billing-support`, `resolve-admin-ops-cockpit`, and `app/admin/ops/page` tests, then full workspace lint/format/typecheck gates.

- **ADR-079 active-skill state overwrite hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, docs; routing reliability 2026-05-04):** fixed the founder-reported live regression where background skill-routing check #2 was already deciding `useSkills=false`, but the chat could still stay on the old active Skill until a later routing cycle. The issue turned out to be state consistency, not cadence: newer background `inactive` routing truth could be overwritten later by stale older turn state. `AutoSkillRoutingStateService` now guards both `persistFromTurnRouting()` and `markBackgroundCheckQueued()` against backwards writes, so an older `active` payload can no longer overwrite a newer `inactive` state with a higher `checkedAtMessageIndex`, and equal-index `active` state cannot resurrect an already persisted `inactive` result. The earlier local foreground-threshold experiment was removed; user turns still answer at normal speed and background routing remains background-only. Verification: focused `auto-skill-routing-state`, `turn-routing`, `turn-execution`, `send-web-chat-turn`, and `stream-web-chat-turn` regressions plus changed-file diagnostics.

- **ADR-084 Slice 6 immediate activation/materialization (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-04):** trusted paid-success lifecycle transitions now do more than mark assistants dirty. `ManageWorkspaceSubscriptionLifecycleService` now triggers immediate workspace assistant rematerialization plus runtime/provider warmup for published assistants after paid activation/recovery/renewal success, so the updated plan/runtime bundle is prepared right after the lifecycle write instead of waiting for a later incidental refresh. The chat return path now carries `billingPaymentIntentId` and reloads server truth from the authoritative payment-intent/bootstrap boundaries on successful return, which helps the user land in paid-ready state as soon as trusted success has actually landed without promoting client return params into billing truth. Verification: focused lifecycle/materialization API tests, focused chat return test, then full lint/format/API+web typecheck gates.

- **ADR-084 Slice 5 webhook-to-lifecycle integration (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-04):** added a production billing secret surface under `Admin > Tools` for PersAI-managed encrypted billing-provider credentials, starting with the CloudPayments API Secret. Added trusted CloudPayments webhook ingress under `/api/v1/public/billing/cloudpayments/webhooks/:notificationType` with HMAC verification over the raw request body, minimal `check` validation, payment-intent status mutation (`pending_confirmation`, `succeeded`, `failed`, `canceled`, `reversed`), and ADR-083 lifecycle application through the existing `ApplyWorkspaceSubscriptionBillingEventService` instead of client return state. The current checkout start still uses the `manual_test` adapter, so the webhook/lifecycle path is now ready ahead of the later concrete CloudPayments checkout/session adapter slice. Verification: focused billing credential + CloudPayments webhook tests, full lint/format/API+web typecheck gates, and full workspace test suite.

- **ADR-084 Slice 4 checkout and return flow (`apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-04):** wired logged-in pricing cards to the PersAI-owned `POST /api/v1/assistant/billing/payment-intents` boundary instead of support mailto fallback, with explicit `card` and `sbp_qr` starts from the shared pricing UI. Added a bounded `/app/billing/checkout/[paymentIntentId]` handoff screen that reads the persisted payment intent, launches the current `manual_test` checkout contour, and returns users to `/app/chat` with a normalized `success` / `failed` / `pending` envelope. Chat now shows a quiet billing return banner that explains retry/pending semantics without activating the paid plan early; trusted provider/webhook confirmation and lifecycle mutation remain the next ADR-084 slice. Verification: focused pricing/checkout/chat web tests, web typecheck, then workspace lint/format/typecheck gates.

- **ADR-079 auto-skill routing bootstrap + prompt compaction hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, docs; routing reliability 2026-05-04):** fixed the founder-reported regression where auto-skill routing could keep retriggering on every turn after message `3` if `autoSkillRoutingState` stayed `null`, even though `Admin > Runtime` correctly stored `3 / 5`. `AutoSkillRoutingStateService.shouldRunBackgroundCheck()` now treats `null` bootstrap as an exact first-check at message `3`, and queued background checks now advance `checkedAtMessageIndex` plus reset `messageCountSinceCheck` to `0` so failed/in-flight checks do not leave chats in a pseudo-bootstrap loop. Runtime classifier-failure handling for forced Skill-routing checks now persists a dormant inactive checked state instead of preserving `null`, which moves the chat back onto the normal 5-message cadence after a bad classifier turn. Also compacted the skill-routing classifier prompt for overflow safety: enabled Skills are now summarized in a short capped list (`id`, short `name`, `category`, short `tags`, `+N more enabled skills`), recent routing context is limited to the last 6 messages at 120 chars each, `Current auto Skill state` no longer dumps the long `topicSummary`, and the router system prompt now explicitly asks for compact JSON with a brief snake_case `reasonCode`. Verification: focused `auto-skill-routing-state`, `turn-routing`, `send-web-chat-turn`, `stream-web-chat-turn`, and `turn-execution` regressions plus changed-file diagnostics.

- **ADR-084 Slice 3 payment-intent and provider-port foundation (`apps/api`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-04):** added the first PersAI-owned checkout foundation before any real provider wiring. Prisma now persists `workspace_payment_intents` with action/status/payment-method snapshots, checkout mode/payload, idempotency key, return URL, provider refs, and error state; `POST /api/v1/assistant/billing/payment-intents` creates or reuses that intent for a visible paid plan and returns a provider-neutral checkout payload, while `GET /api/v1/assistant/billing/payment-intents/:paymentIntentId` reads the persisted state back for the same user. The existing billing-provider port now creates normalized checkout sessions and the current dev/manual adapter returns a bounded `manual_test` session instead of pretending a real provider already exists. Downgrade/cancel policy, webhook activation, and checkout UI/return banners remain later ADR-084 slices. Verification: focused payment-intent service + auth-route tests, Prisma/contracts generation, workspace lint/format/typecheck gates.

- **ADR-079 ordinary source priority + preset cleanup (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; production retrieval boundary 2026-05-04):** ordinary non-skill turns now follow runtime-owned staged retrieval driven by an explicit `ordinarySourcePriorityMode` on the retrieval plan (`personal_first`, `product_first`, `web_first`, `mixed_ambiguous`, `not_applicable`). The router resolves the mode in the existing precheck/optional-classifier path using short hardcoded defaults plus admin-overridable term lists (`Admin > Runtime > Router Policy` now exposes `Personal-first priority`, `Product-first priority`, `Web-first priority` textareas), so no new per-turn LLM call was added. API orchestrated retrieval translates the mode into a stage-priority merge over `skill -> user -> product` and emits `policyState=ordinary_personal_first | ordinary_product_first | ordinary_web_first | ordinary_mixed_ambiguous` to retrieval observability so non-skill turns are as auditable as active-skill turns. Prompt presets are no longer modeled as a knowledge source: `preset` was dropped from runtime tool projection, runtime-knowledge-access config, API `read-assistant-knowledge.service`, retrieval observability tracked sources, OpenAPI/contracts/admin client types, and the `KnowledgeRetrievalEventSource` Prisma enum, with a focused migration that also clears any historical `preset` telemetry rows. The Admin Prompts editor and runtime prompt materialization are unaffected. Verification: focused runtime ordinary-mode and API staged-retrieval regressions, with the workspace lint/format/typecheck gates run as the closing step of the slice.

- **ADR-079 active-skill retrieval runtime ownership (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, docs; production retrieval boundary 2026-05-04):** active-skill retrieval is now enforced as a runtime-owned staged policy instead of a prompt hint. The runtime derives a per-turn knowledge source policy from the route decision and sticky active-skill state, narrows model-visible `knowledge_search` / `knowledge_fetch` sources during active-skill turns, and blocks model attempts to reach product/global fallback sources unless the turn explicitly carries product intent. API orchestrated retrieval now runs active-skill turns in stage order (`skill` first, then user-owned knowledge only when Skill grounding is insufficient, then optional web/product escalation) and preserves stage priority when merging context so product/global snippets cannot outrank Skill grounding by default. Retrieval observability recent-event state now exposes policy outcomes such as `skill_only` and escalation states alongside the existing sticky Skill decision metrics.

- **ADR-084 media-card cleanup in Assistant Settings (`apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-04):** removed the now-duplicated `Monthly media` block from `Assistant Settings -> Limits & Plan` and kept the new top media summary cards as the single monthly-media surface. The shared metric-card layout now reserves a consistent title area so long headings can wrap without pushing usage values like `3/5` or `9/10` out of visual alignment across the desktop row. Verification: focused `assistant-settings` web test and changed-file diagnostics.

- **ADR-079 production review hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `apps/web`, docs; routing/retrieval prod cleanup 2026-05-04):** closed the independent review tails after the smart skill routing + sticky retrieval slice. Skill-routing cadence now remains effective for assistants with enabled Skills even when the early router toggle is off, so admin cadence UI no longer drifts away from runtime behavior. Admin Skill mutations now proactively clear persisted chat routing/retrieval state, sticky retrieval observability no longer inflates mode counters with non-Skill searches/fetches, and the adaptive retrieval policy now only trusts prior no-op helper signals when the candidate set hash stays stable. Verification: focused runtime/API regressions, workspace lint, format check, and API/web typechecks.

- **ADR-079 smart skill routing + sticky retrieval (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; production routing efficiency 2026-05-04):** moved the founder-requested auto-skill cadence knobs into global `Admin > Runtime` settings (`skillRoutingInitialCheckUserMessageIndex`, `skillRoutingBackgroundRecheckIntervalMessages`) and wired them through API/OpenAPI/contracts/UI so new-chat bootstrap and later background rechecks are admin-owned policy instead of code constants. Added a separate per-chat `skillRetrievalState` on `assistant_chats` plus adaptive retrieval policy logic that chooses `reuse_cached_refs`, `refresh_search_only`, or `refresh_with_helper` inside an already-active Skill. Close same-topic follow-ups can now reuse cached Skill refs or skip the helper rerank when raw search is already clear, while topic drift or ambiguity still refreshes properly. Retrieval observability now records decision mode, cache-reuse hits, helper reorder behavior, and candidate-quality signals so the new sticky path is measurable rather than hidden.

- **ADR-079 auto-skill routing cadence hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, docs; routing regression 2026-05-04):** reconciled the live founder expectations with the actual auto-skill routing code path. Skill assignment changes now reset per-chat auto-skill state deterministically instead of leaving stale/null chat state behind, brand-new chats wait until the 3rd user message for the first background Skill check, ordinary turns preserve/increment inactive cadence state instead of erasing it back to `null`, and API background checks now rebuild their post-turn routing context from the persisted latest state plus the freshest chat tail. Runtime auto-skill activation no longer foreground-classifies ordinary no-state turns from lexical/high-signal hints; initial activation/drift rechecks are cadence-owned background checks, while already-active Skills still reuse sticky state between those checks. Verification: focused API/runtime routing regressions, workspace lint, format check, and API/web typechecks.

- **ADR-084 Slice 2 limits/media summary + pricing scroll polish (`apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-04):** refined the remaining pricing/settings UI tail after the pricing-page launch. `Assistant Settings -> Limits & Plan` now uses the top summary row for enabled monthly media tools (`image_generate`, `image_edit`, `video_generate`) instead of `Active chats` / storage buckets, while those secondary quota cards moved into the collapsed `Tool limits` block. The public `/pricing` route now uses a contained scroll surface so tariff scrolling feels like the chat/app surface rather than a separate browser-page scrollbar. Verification: focused `assistant-settings` + `pricing-page-view` tests, workspace lint, format check, and API/web typechecks.

- **Web chat send auth latency hardening (`apps/web`, docs; chat speed 2026-05-04):** reduced ordinary web chat send latency by removing the forced fresh Clerk-token round-trip from every message. `apps/web/app/app/_components/use-chat.ts` now starts normal chat sends with cached `getToken()` and retries the stream once with `getToken({ skipCache: true })` only when the first pre-headers stream attempt fails with HTTP `401`, preserving auth recovery without making every message wait on a fresh token. Verification: focused `use-chat` web regression, web typecheck, and changed-file diagnostics.

- **Clerk redirect hardening for pricing/auth routes (`apps/web`, docs; auth stability 2026-05-04):** fixed the custom auth-screen redirect drift that could drop protected-route intent while switching between `sign-in`, `sign-up`, forgot-password, and the Clerk `sso-callback` path. `apps/web/app/lib/clerk-navigation.ts` now exposes a shared safe redirect-preservation helper, auth-screen links carry validated `redirect_url` forward, sign-up completion respects a safe redirect target instead of always hardcoding `/app/setup`, and `sso-callback` now finalizes/recovers into the same safe target or preserves it when falling back to `sign-in`. Verification: focused sign-in, sign-up, and SSO callback web tests.

- **Web desktop send UX hardening (`apps/web`, docs; chat UX 2026-05-04):** audited the founder-reported delay/focus regression around web chat sends and confirmed the visible spinner lag was partly UI-authored rather than backend latency. The off-bubble `sending` indicator now appears after `250 ms` instead of `1000 ms`, and desktop composer focus is restored more reliably across the `Send -> Stop` rerender path by keeping a short post-send refocus window and preventing desktop pointer-down on the send/stop button from stealing focus before the textarea reclaim runs. Verification: focused `chat-input` / `chat-message` web regressions, web typecheck, and changed-file diagnostics.

- **ADR-084 Slice 2 pricing page delivery (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-04):** finished the real pricing surface on top of the earlier Admin Plans groundwork. Added `GET /api/v1/public/plans/pricing` as the guest-safe plan-card source, built shared mobile-first pricing UI for both `/pricing` and `/app/pricing`, wired `Assistant Settings` `Change plan` into the in-app route, moved the landing `Plans` link to the new public page, and kept Capacitor Back behavior correct by routing signed-in pricing through `AppShell` while mounting the existing back bridge on the public page. Pricing cards now show localized admin-owned copy, current-plan state, and quiet fact chips derived from real plan limits while direct checkout remains the next ADR-084 slice.

- **ADR-079 background skill recheck force-check fix (`apps/api`, docs; routing regression 2026-05-04):** fixed the live regression where enabling a Skill could keep ordinary later web turns on `sticky_skill_reuse` every message instead of letting the background scheduler re-check topic drift only on the documented cadence. Sync and stream web chat paths now mark background Skill-routing checks with `forceCheck=true` before calling the runtime `skill-routing-check` endpoint, so the every-5-message background pass actually re-runs the classifier instead of reusing the previously active Skill. Verification: focused `send-web-chat-turn` and `stream-web-chat-turn` API regressions plus changed-file diagnostics.

- **ADR-084 pricing presentation fields in Admin Plans (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-04):** added the Slice 2 groundwork so pricing cards can be generated from `Admin > Plans` instead of hard-coded marketing data. Plans now expose a public `presentation` block with show-on-pricing toggle, display order, highlighted-card flag, structured price, localized RU/EN title/subtitle/notes/badge/CTA, and localized feature-bullet arrays. The admin plan API persists that data through `billingProviderHints.presentation`, OpenAPI/contracts expose it, and `Admin > Plans` now edits it with local guards for visible pricing cards.

- **ADR-084 billing visibility UI polish (`apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** redesigned the user-facing billing summary in `Assistant Settings` and the sidebar account popup for the mobile-first billing pass. `Limits & Plan` now emphasizes the token budget, moves `Active chats`, `Media storage`, and `Knowledge storage` into compact summary cards, removes the visible plan code, and reuses shared localized lifecycle/date copy (`Trial until`, `Next billing`, etc.). `Monthly media` now hides disabled media tools entirely, while `Tool limits` is collapsed by default and expands into the full enabled-then-disabled tool catalog, including media tools and tools without explicit daily caps.

- **ADR-082 quota-status monthly media visibility (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** fixed the assistant-facing `quota_status` path so media billing truth is no longer mixed into legacy daily tool rows. Internal runtime quota status now excludes `image_generate`, `image_edit`, and `video_generate` from `tools[]`, leaving their paid-usage truth only in `monthlyMediaQuotas`, and the model-facing tool descriptions now explicitly distinguish non-media daily counters from monthly media limits.

- **ADR-083 legacy-user lifecycle normalization (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** added an explicit `Initialize lifecycle now` support action in `Admin > Ops` for old assistants that still resolve only through the legacy assistant quota-plan fallback and therefore have no real billing status/dates/support buttons. The action creates the first real `WorkspaceSubscription` from the current registration policy using the current time, clears the stale `assistantGovernance.quotaPlanCode` fallback pointer, and refreshes the selected cockpit detail plus top user directory so the migrated user immediately becomes testable through the normal lifecycle support path.

- **Admin Ops tester plan selector cleanup (`apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** fixed the old `Admin > Ops` tester/manual `Plan Control` dropdown so it no longer looks blank when plan-catalog state and the current override diverge. The page now keeps the full admin plan catalog in memory, derives active override options at render time, preserves an existing inactive override as explicit cleanup state instead of silently dropping it, and blocks reapplying inactive legacy overrides. Added focused web regression coverage for the option-resolution helper.

- **ADR-084 slice-order doc reconciliation (`docs`; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** reconciled the active ADR-084 execution order after ADR-083 verification. The ADR ledger still shows only Slice 1 complete, so `SESSION-HANDOFF` and ADR-084 current continuation state now point to Slice 2 (`pricing cards from Admin Plans`) as the next honest slice instead of prematurely jumping to Slice 3 (`payment intent and provider port`).

- **ADR-083 billing lifecycle reminder reschedule hardening (`apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** fixed the post-Slice-6/7 drift where `trial_extended` and `grace_extended` could create duplicate future billing reminder jobs instead of rescheduling the existing reminder work to the new lifecycle window. `ScheduleBillingLifecycleNotificationsService` now reuses the active pending/skipped `trial_ending` and `grace_ending` jobs per channel when lifecycle windows move, and broader `ClerkAuthMiddleware` regression coverage now pins `stage-attachment`, direct chat attachment upload, voice transcription, and Files cache cleanup alongside the already-fixed billing support action route.

- **ADR-083 Slice 6 admin Ops support actions (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** finished the Ops Cockpit support slice by replacing the generic workspace-subscription buttons with real lifecycle-native admin actions. Admins can now extend trial, grant/extend grace, send a billing reminder, apply fallback immediately, and restore paid manually when prior paid-period truth exists; each action appends `source=admin` lifecycle history, updates the selected Ops detail after success, and reuses the existing notification job pipeline instead of inventing a separate admin billing truth.

- **ADR-083 Slice 7 billing provider integration readiness (`apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** added a PersAI-owned `workspace_subscription_billing_events` snapshot layer for trusted provider/admin/manual payment inputs and routed those inputs through one lifecycle application service before they touch `WorkspaceSubscription`. Paid activation, renewal success, renewal failure, payment recovery, and reversal/fallback now reuse the existing lifecycle/materialization/quota/notification chain, provider sync no longer deletes local subscription truth on missing provider data, and explicit admin paid-period activation now uses the same event-driven path instead of raw subscription row mutation.

- **ADR-083 Slice 6 live-test hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** closed the post-slice authorization/UI gaps before live testing. `GET/PUT /api/v1/admin/billing/lifecycle-settings` are now explicitly covered by Clerk bearer middleware, the Ops user directory now enforces admin read authorization before returning users, per-user Ops reapply checks admin access before acting on another user, and the Ops workspace-subscription button no longer forces `status=active` so trial-plan assignments can use the service-owned trial defaults. Reapply buttons now stop row-click propagation and surface failed per-user reapply responses instead of silently refreshing.

- **ADR-083 Slice 6 Ops Cockpit billing support UX (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** made `Admin > Ops` useful for subscription support without reading provider state directly. The top user directory now focuses on email, plan, billing status, next relevant billing/trial/grace date, usage risk, and actions. The selected cockpit detail now exposes PersAI-owned subscription state, trial/grace/current-period windows, provider refs, current quota period, latest lifecycle events, and latest billing notification jobs. The previously raw `/admin/ops/users` response is now in the OpenAPI contract.

- **ADR-083 Slice 5 notification settings and lifecycle outbox (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** completed the durable billing lifecycle notification foundation. `Admin > Billing Settings` now persists notification policy with required email, optional assistant push, and admin-owned rule/offset settings. Lifecycle events create durable `billing_lifecycle_notification_jobs` for required email work and optional assistant notification work; assistant push reuses the existing `assistant_notification_outbox` with `billing_lifecycle` source and static required-facts copy. Because no real email adapter exists yet, email work is stored as pending notification jobs rather than falsely marked delivered.

- **ADR-083 Slice 4 billing-period quota reset foundation (`apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** completed the subscription-period quota foundation for paid lifecycle readiness. Credits/token budget and monthly media quota snapshots now share one recurring quota period resolver that prefers `WorkspaceSubscription.currentPeriodStartedAt/currentPeriodEndsAt`, including payment-recovery periods created by Slice 3, and falls back to UTC calendar months only when subscription period truth is absent. Inbound token-budget enforcement, abuse quota-pressure, Ops Cockpit quota detail, and Business Cockpit quota-pressure distribution now read the current `workspace_token_budget_period_counters` bucket instead of stale compatibility `WorkspaceQuotaAccountingState.tokenBudgetUsed`, so a recovered paid billing period starts from the new period counter rather than old-period usage.

- **ADR-083 Slice 3 subscription lifecycle state machine (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** completed the production subscription lifecycle foundation. Effective subscription resolution creates a real `WorkspaceSubscription` from the active default registration plan, trial registrations get real trial/current-period windows, expired trials apply the plan-owned trial fallback, and paid renewal failure/grace/recovery now run through a dedicated lifecycle service with append-only lifecycle events. `Admin > Billing Settings` owns persisted `gracePeriodDays` and global fallback plan policy, Admin Plans can set plan-level `paidFallbackPlanCode`, grace keeps paid access/limits active, grace expiry applies plan/global fallback without hard-coded Free, and payment recovery restores active paid state with a new billing period for ADR-082 quota truth.

- **ADR-083 Slice 2 plan lifecycle policy fields (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** Admin Plans now owns `lifecyclePolicy.trialFallbackPlanCode` for trial plans. Trial plan saves require an admin-selected fallback plan, API writes reject missing, self-referential, or inactive fallback references, plan reads/writes preserve the lifecycle policy in `billingProviderHints`, and user plan visibility exposes the effective plan's trial fallback code. The Admin Plans editor shows an explicit fallback-plan selector beside trial duration and no longer auto-fills a hard-coded trial duration when Trial is toggled on.

- **ADR-085 controlled materialization rollouts (`docs`; control-plane hardening 2026-05-03):** accepted the target-state decision to retire the old `Admin > Rollouts` JSON governance patch workflow from the active product path and replace `/admin/rollouts` with an operational dashboard for controlled materialization rollouts. Manual `Reapply all` becomes a queued `manual_reapply` rollout instead of a synchronous all-assistant loop, global changes create visible scoped rollout jobs automatically, and materialization/runtime bundle propagation must use concurrency limits, per-assistant locks, retry/backoff, skip-if-fresh, progress bars, failed-item visibility, and retry/cancel controls. ADR-085 implementation must start with a code audit and produce one coherent production path, not a temporary hybrid where old JSON rollouts and new materialization rollouts both act as product truth.

- **Runtime quota-status media snapshot (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`; production media quota 2026-05-03):** fixed the assistant-facing `quota_status` tool so image generation/editing/video generation are no longer reported only through legacy daily tool fields. The internal quota status response now includes the current monthly media quota snapshot (`usedUnits`, `limitUnits`, `remainingUnits`, period, and reconciliation fields), letting assistants answer media-limit questions from ADR-082 monthly quota truth instead of saying media tools have no daily limit.

- **Web media stop hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/web`; production media quota 2026-05-03):** fixed explicit user Stop behavior for long web tool turns. The web client now waits briefly for the hard-stop request to reach the API before aborting the local SSE, preventing avoidable `web_turn_inflight` conflicts on the next prompt, and terminal interrupted/failed turns clear live tool/thinking activity so the UI does not remain in a "thinking" state. Explicit user stop after generated media artifacts are returned now settles the reserved media quota as user-caused cost, while server/runtime/delivery failures still reconcile or release without counting as successful delivery.

- **ADR-082 production risk closure (`apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** closed the paid-launch quota risks found after Slice 6. Credits/token budget now persists in `workspace_token_budget_period_counters` keyed by the effective subscription/calendar period, with `WorkspaceQuotaAccountingState.tokenBudgetUsed` kept only as a current-period compatibility snapshot. API media delivery entry points now mark reserved generated artifacts as reconciliation-required when runtime returned media but sync web, stream web, or Telegram delivery is not reached/completed, preventing orphaned monthly media reservations from silently charging or blocking users.

- **ADR-083/084 billing transition invariants (`docs`; production billing readiness 2026-05-03):** tightened the cross-ADR billing lifecycle contract so payment/admin events flow through PersAI payment intent, `WorkspaceSubscription`, effective plan resolution, ADR-082 quota snapshots, config generation/materialization, UI/runtime visibility, and lifecycle-derived notifications in one strict order. ADR-084 now also records the refund/chargeback quota edge: fallback/free applies immediately, already consumed paid-period Credits/media are not silently rolled back, and Ops should retain audit/risk context for future finance/support policy.

- **ADR-082 product cleanup and launch hardening (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** completed the post-settlement cleanup so active UI no longer presents image generation/editing/video generation as daily paid quota. User Settings now displays delivery-confirmed monthly media allowances, web and messenger quota errors distinguish monthly media exhaustion from daily safety caps, API plan visibility excludes media tools from daily tool-limit rows, and Admin Plans hides daily cap editing for media tools while keeping per-turn media caps as safety controls. ADR-082 is now completed through Slice 6; daily counters remain safety/rate-limit state only.

- **ADR-082 delivery-confirmed media settlement (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** moved `image_generate`, `image_edit`, and `video_generate` off pre-provider daily quota consumption and onto subscription-period monthly reservation. Runtime now reserves monthly media units before expensive provider work, releases reservations when provider work yields no deliverable artifact, and tags generated artifacts with their source media tool. API monthly media counters now support reserve, settle, release, and reconciliation-required mutations; `MediaDeliveryService` settles only after persisted/channel-delivered media succeeds and marks provider-output/no-delivery failures for admin reconciliation instead of charging user quota.

- **ADR-084 billing provider readiness (`docs`; production billing readiness 2026-05-03):** accepted the target-state decision for provider-ready pricing, checkout, payment intents, webhook lifecycle updates, admin manual payments, assistant payment links/QR, and immediate upgrade activation. Pricing cards are generated from selected Admin Plans, guests and logged-in users share one pricing page, first production payment methods are card and SBP QR, Plan Control remains tester/admin override rather than billing truth, upgrades activate immediately while downgrades/cancellations apply at period end, and concrete provider integration is reduced to an adapter/webhook wiring task.

- **ADR-082 monthly media quota model (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** added plan-owned subscription-period monthly limits for `image_generate`, `image_edit`, and `video_generate` units, plus `workspace_media_monthly_quota_counters` for reserved/settled/released/reconciliation-required media usage. Admin Plans now edits the monthly media allowances, plan visibility can expose monthly media quota snapshots, and the period resolves from workspace subscription boundaries with an explicit UTC calendar-month fallback for local/manual states. Delivery-confirmed settlement has since landed in Slice 5.

- **ADR-082 weighted token accounting (`apps/api`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** replaced estimator-first token quota recording for completed native turns with provider-metered weighted Credits from runtime `usageAccounting.entries`. API turn facades now carry `usageAccounting` through sync web, streaming web, and Telegram completed turns; quota events resolve model weights from Admin Runtime provider/model profiles, apply cached-input weight separately from ordinary input, round weighted Credits up to the integer quota counter, and mark estimator fallback explicitly when runtime usage is missing. Verification: focused quota accounting regression and API typecheck.

- **ADR-083 subscription lifecycle and billing ops (`docs`; production billing readiness 2026-05-03):** accepted the production target-state decision for registration, trial, paid renewal, global grace, fallback plans, lifecycle notifications, and Admin Ops subscription support. Trial plans must select their fallback plan, paid renewal failure enters global grace while keeping paid access/limits, lifecycle notifications require email with optional guarded assistant push/Telegram, and Ops Cockpit should become the compact user-directory plus detailed subscription/quota/support surface for admin operations.

- **ADR-082 provider/model token profiles (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, docs; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** extended Admin Runtime provider settings from capability-only model id lists to typed provider/model profile rows with `inputTokenWeight`, `cachedInputTokenWeight`, and `outputTokenWeight`. Existing catalog JSON is normalized into neutral default-weight profiles, `availableModelsByProvider` remains the chat-model alias, media/model selection paths now read capabilities from profiles, and Admin Runtime shows token-weight previews for selected runtime/router models. Verification: contract generation, focused API/web tests, contracts/API/web typechecks.

- **ADR-082 billing-period reset truth (`docs`; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** clarified that `WorkspaceSubscription.currentPeriodStartedAt/currentPeriodEndsAt` is the shared reset boundary for all paid recurring quotas, including Credits/token budget, monthly media limits, future paid quotas, renewal visibility, and future billing-provider notification/reminder automation. Daily counters remain only for true day-scoped safety/rate-limit controls.

- **ADR-082 continuation handoff polish (`docs`; billing readiness 2026-05-03):** updated ADR-082 with explicit implementation status, coarse reviewable slices, execution rules, and a ready-to-use prompt for starting the next session on Slice 2 provider/model token profiles in `Admin > Runtime`.

- **Admin Plans active chat cap restoration (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`; plan quota UI 2026-05-03):** restored the per-plan `activeWebChatsLimit` field in `Admin > Plans` under Plan limits / Quota limits, persisted it through `billingProviderHints.quotaAccounting`, exposed it in the admin plan API contract, and made web chat creation/media staging use the effective plan limit instead of only `WEB_ACTIVE_CHATS_CAP`. Verification: focused admin-plan/media/quota tests, contract generation/typecheck, workspace lint, format check, and API/web typechecks.

- **ADR-082 billing quota and delivery-confirmed media accounting (`docs`; production billing readiness 2026-05-03):** accepted the target-state decision for provider/model-weighted token quota and delivery-confirmed monthly media generation accounting. Token budget becomes one user-facing `Credits` metric derived from all provider/runtime `usageAccounting` entries in a turn, with provider/model token weights owned by `Admin > Runtime`. Image generation, image edit, and video generation move from daily caps to plan-owned monthly limits, and media quota is settled only after successful user-visible delivery; provider-cost/no-delivery cases require admin reconciliation instead of charging user quota.

- **Delivered image markdown fallback fix (`apps/api`; live web chat media UX 2026-05-03):** fixed the final delivery sanitizer so a model-written delivered image markdown line like `![board_concept_diagram](attachment://<fileRef>)` is removed when the image attachment card was actually delivered, even if the link text omits `.png`. Live logs for assistant `275e2382-cb4e-41d8-98da-fe04d4569f55` showed image generation succeeded, but the streamed markdown produced a transient/broken inline fallback before the real attachment card appeared. Verification: focused final-delivery-honesty regression and changed-file diagnostics.

- **ADR-079 grounded Skill/user-KB routing guard (`apps/runtime`, `apps/api`, `apps/provider-gateway`; context overflow hardening 2026-05-03):** heavy grounded turns that combine selected Skills with user KB/files now get a runtime premium-reply floor even if the router classifier says `normal`, while ordinary no-Skill/no-retrieval turns keep normal routing. Runtime also replans injected `Retrieved Knowledge Context` against the plan-managed `knowledgeHydrationBudget`, attempts one LLM recovery pass after provider context-window overflow, and maps provider context-window errors to a distinct `runtime_context_window_exceeded` path instead of generic `runtime_unreachable`. Verification: focused runtime routing/execution regressions and API stream mapping regression.

- **Global admin Knowledge ownership (`apps/api`, docs; platform KB migration 2026-05-03):** promoted Skill, Product KB, and platform/global Knowledge to platform/admin-managed shared KB ownership instead of tenant workspace-owned rows. Shared source/chunk tables no longer carry tenant `workspaceId`; shared indexing jobs and vector chunks use `workspaceId=NULL` with migration checks; Product Overview/Principles are deduplicated to single platform Product KB entries; admin services no longer filter shared KB by `access.workspaceId` or charge tenant knowledge quota; runtime retrieval keeps Skill access constrained by active assignments while searching selected Skill ids/source types without the assistant workspace source filter. Verification: focused admin/retrieval/indexing/authorization/audit tests plus API typecheck.

- **ADR-079 Skill document retrieval workspace fix (`apps/api`; live Dietitian grounding 2026-05-03):** fixed the production retrieval gap where an assigned platform/admin Skill could be selected (`useSkills=true`, active Skill state) but its documents were not injected because Skill pgvector search incorrectly filtered vector chunks by the assistant workspace. Skill vector search now remains constrained by validated active `skillIds` and Skill source types, but does not require the vector rows to belong to the assistant workspace, matching the platform Skill catalog model. Live inspection for assistant `7fd2c02c-6663-4691-977d-feb3f706836d` showed 0 vector rows with the assistant-workspace filter and 291 rows without it for the active Dietitian Skill. Verification: focused orchestrated retrieval regression, API typecheck, changed-file diagnostics, and live SQL root-cause check.

- **ADR-080 admin locale dropdowns (`apps/web`; admin authoring UX 2026-05-03):** replaced free-text locale inputs for Skill Knowledge cards and Product KB text entries with fixed dropdowns (`Any locale`, `en`, `en-US`, `ru`, `ru-RU`) so admins choose consistent locale values while `Any locale` still saves as `null`. Verification: focused Admin Skills/Admin Knowledge helper tests and changed-file diagnostics.

- **ADR-080 assistant-proposed Skill cards save fix (`apps/web`; admin authoring UX 2026-05-03):** fixed the Admin Skills authoring flow so assistant-proposed Skill knowledge cards are not left as unsaved UI-only proposals when the admin presses the main Skill `Save`. The main save now persists all new assistant-proposed cards as draft Knowledge cards, and the proposal panel also has an explicit `Save all proposed` action with dedupe against already saved card content. Verification: focused Admin Skills helper test, web lint/typecheck, and repo format check.

- **ADR-079 Skill routing metadata-match fix (`apps/runtime`; live Dietitian routing 2026-05-03):** fixed the runtime precheck gap where short domain-specific turns could finish as `simple_turn` with `retrievalPlan.useSkills=false` even when the enabled Skill metadata clearly matched the conversation. The precheck now uses the enabled Skill metadata already materialized from admin-managed Skill fields (`name`, `description`, `tags`, `routingExamples`) plus the recent user/assistant chat window as a generic classifier trigger, with no domain vocabulary hard-coded into runtime. Existing active Skill sticky reuse still stays fast unless forced/high-signal recheck applies. Verification: focused runtime routing regression plus runtime typecheck, repo format, lint, and API/web typechecks.

- **ADR-080 assistant Skill authoring schema hotfix (`apps/api`; live authoring 2026-05-03):** fixed `skill_authoring_draft` structured output schema for OpenAI strict mode by requiring every object property and representing optional draft fields as nullable values. This addresses live provider-gateway failures where OpenAI rejected the schema with `Invalid schema for response_format 'skill_authoring_draft': object schema missing properties`. Verification: focused authoring draft regression plus API lint/typecheck.

- **ADR-080 Product KB baseline consolidation (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `packages/runtime-contract`, `docs`; admin-managed product truth 2026-05-03):** moved PersAI Product Overview and PersAI Product Principles out of hidden runtime hard-code and into seeded/backfilled active Product KB text entries. Runtime product retrieval now uses admin-managed Product KB entries/files plus plan/subscription catalog facts for tariffs and quotas; non-pricing product truth is not injected from code-owned documents. Orchestrated retrieval labels product grounding as `product_kb` and stops treating prompt presets as a separate Product Knowledge source.

- **ADR-080 assistant Skill authoring (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `packages/contracts`, `docs`; admin draft enrichment 2026-05-03):** added the admin-only `POST /api/v1/admin/skills/:skillId/authoring/draft` flow for `Собрать с помощью агента`. The flow is control-plane/admin authoring, not ordinary runtime chat: API calls provider-gateway text generation with the admin Knowledge `authoringModelKey` slot and falls back to the platform primary chat model when unset. Admin Skills can fill editable draft fields and load assistant-proposed knowledge cards as draft-only editor content; nothing is saved, activated, indexed, or exposed to runtime retrieval without explicit admin save/activation. Verification: focused authoring/API and admin UI regressions plus workspace lint, format check, API/web typechecks, and full workspace tests.

- **ADR-080 admin authoring UI (`apps/web`, `docs`; production-readiness 2026-05-02):** added admin UI surfaces for Product KB text entries under Admin Knowledge and Skill knowledge cards inside Admin Skills detail. Both surfaces are draft-first, expose explicit lifecycle selection (`draft`, `active`, `stale`, `archived`), only show reindex for active persisted entries, and archive authored entries without treating them as Files. Web API wrappers now cover the ADR-080 authored-entry endpoints, with focused helper tests for payload shaping and lifecycle summaries. Verification: focused admin Knowledge/Skills page tests and web typecheck; full AGENTS gates follow.

- **ADR-080 authored knowledge implementation slice (`apps/api`, `packages/contracts`, `docs`; admin Knowledge/Skills model 2026-05-02):** added native API/data model support for admin-authored Skill knowledge cards and Product KB text entries with `draft` / `active` / `stale` / `archived` lifecycle and provenance metadata. Active entries now enqueue and process through the existing ADR-079 `KnowledgeIndexingJob` pipeline, while draft/archived entries are excluded from indexing and runtime retrieval revalidates `active` + `ready` state before injecting Skill or Product KB context. Product text entries live under admin Knowledge; Skill cards live under admin Skill detail. This slice intentionally does not add assistant-assisted authoring endpoints, crawling, curator jobs, marketplace features, or versioned Skills. Verification: focused API service/indexing regressions plus workspace lint, format check, API typecheck, and web typecheck.

- **Chat quick-action minimal spacing polish (`apps/web`, `docs`; UI follow-up 2026-05-02):** simplified the assistant quick-action block under chat replies so it no longer renders as a heavy gradient/sidebar panel. Actions now appear as a quiet vertical list with more whitespace and a small per-action arrow marker, closer to the lightweight ChatGPT-style suggestions shown in the founder reference. Verification: focused chat-message regressions plus workspace lint, format check, API typecheck, and web typecheck.

- **ADR-080 admin-controlled Knowledge authoring (`docs`; architecture decision 2026-05-02):** accepted ADR-080 for admin-controlled Skill knowledge cards, Product KB text entries, and assistant-assisted Skill draft/enrichment. The decision keeps admins in control: generated drafts can fill or enrich Skill fields and propose knowledge cards, but active runtime knowledge requires explicit admin save/activation and indexes through the existing ADR-079 pipeline. Verification: repository format check.

- **Files send ambiguity and honesty live fix (`apps/runtime`, `apps/api`, `docs`; live file-delivery fix 2026-05-02):** fixed the live case where `files.send` was invoked but no attachment card appeared because the model selected an existing file by name/query and the registry returned duplicate visible filenames, such as sandbox and runtime copies of the same markdown file. `files.send` now resolves same-visible-name duplicates to the newest match instead of failing as ambiguous, while truly different matches still require refinement. Final delivery honesty now also appends an undelivered correction when it strips a technical `Assistant sent an attachment: ..., fileRef: ...` line but no attachment was delivered. Verification: focused runtime files-tool and API final-delivery-honesty regressions plus workspace lint, format check, API typecheck, and web typecheck.

- **Media generation tool-call guidance (`apps/api`, `docs`; live UX fix 2026-05-02):** tightened `image_generate` and `video_generate` tool catalog guidance so direct user requests to create images/videos require an immediate tool call and explicitly forbid printing `image_generate(...)`, `video_generate(...)`, JSON arguments, or fenced code blocks as a substitute. This addresses first-try failures where the assistant described a media tool invocation instead of starting generation. Verification: focused tool catalog regression, changed-file formatting, and changed-file lints.

- **Assistant actions UI polish (`apps/web`, `docs`; UI follow-up 2026-05-02):** completed assistant-owned background actions now render as compact run-history cards only, without repeating the full task prompt/status/actions, and the list caps completed items at five visible cards. The assistant-actions helper copy is now user-facing rather than internal product rationale. Chat quick-action suggestions now sit in a lightly separated action block with a small marker, and the desktop composer restores focus more aggressively after Enter sends so typing can continue without an extra click. Verification: focused chat input/message regressions plus full workspace lint, format check, API typecheck, and web typecheck.

- **Files delivery first-try polish (`apps/runtime`, `apps/api`, `docs`; live-smoke fix 2026-05-02):** successful `files.send` / `files.write_and_send` results shown back to the model now use a compact delivery-success signal instead of raw `fileRef` metadata, reducing the chance that the model treats tool output text as the final answer. Final delivery honesty also strips technical `Assistant sent an attachment: ..., fileRef: ...` lines if they still appear. Verification: focused runtime sanitizer, runtime turn-execution, and API final-delivery-honesty regressions.

- **ADR-078 closure (`docs`; founder acceptance 2026-05-02):** marked the consolidated follow-through program complete. ADR-079 Knowledge/Skills retrieval, ADR-081 Files architecture, ADR-074 `R2`, mobile shell rollout, and assistant background-task verification are accepted as closed for ADR-078. Idle re-engagement Telegram delivery is documented as conditional `push/no_push` behavior after live inspection showed a deliberate `no_push`, not a delivery failure.

- **Base Skill catalog electronics engineering (`apps/api`, live DB; catalog update 2026-05-02):** added `Electronics Engineer / Инженер-схемотехник` to the Engineering Skill catalog for circuit design, PCB planning, component selection, board bring-up, and electronics debugging. The Skill was also upserted into the live dev database so it is available immediately, with a compatible `⚡️` icon. Base Skill seeding now preserves existing rows instead of overwriting admin edits on every seed run. Verification: focused base-skill seed lint/format checks and live Prisma readback.

- **ADR-079 vector insert timestamp fix (`apps/api`, `docs`; live-smoke fix 2026-05-02):** fixed pgvector indexing persistence for Skill/Product KB chunks by writing `updated_at = now()` on first `knowledge_vector_chunks` insert. Live GKE showed OpenAI embeddings were generated after the credential fix, but PostgreSQL rejected new vector rows with `23502` until the raw insert supplied the required timestamp. Verification: focused vector boundary, indexing worker, and embedding credential regressions.

- **ADR-079 live embedding credential fix (`apps/api`, `docs`; live-smoke fix 2026-05-02):** fixed the live reason Skill/Product reindex jobs still completed with `embeddingChunkCount=0` after admin policy backfill. `KnowledgeEmbeddingService` now uses the normal `openai` provider secret for OpenAI embeddings, matching the active runtime chat provider-key truth instead of looking for a removed memory-search tool key. Missing embedding credentials now emit a warning instead of silently looking like successful vector indexing. Verification: focused embedding credential regression; live GKE/DB inspection confirmed the pre-fix state had the normal `openai` provider secret while Skill document embeddings were still null.

- **ADR-079 Skill/Product semantic retrieval backfill (`apps/api`, `docs`; live-smoke fix 2026-05-02):** admin Knowledge retrieval-policy updates now enqueue backfill reindex jobs for ready Product KB sources and Skill documents that lack `knowledge_vector_chunks` for the configured admin embedding model. Skill orchestrated retrieval now requires vector grounding when an admin embedding model is configured, so lexical-only Skill matches are not injected as if they were semantic grounding. Selected Skill vector hits are expanded through a server-side fetch-window over neighboring `SkillDocumentChunk` rows before context injection, with `skill` fetch telemetry recording non-zero `fetchDepth` / `fetchedChars` for exact excerpt support. Verification: focused admin policy, indexing worker, read-knowledge, orchestration, admin Knowledge/Skills, runtime knowledge/routing/turn tests plus API/runtime typechecks.

- **Skill badge icon color polish (`apps/web`, `docs`; UI follow-up 2026-05-02):** removed the flat grayscale treatment from the Skill emoji inside the strong activity badge. The icon now keeps a softly muted color treatment (`saturate(0.68) brightness(1.04)`) so it feels more premium and legible without becoming a bright glow. Verification: focused activity-badge regression, web typecheck, and changed-file lints.

- **Skills compactness and file-send continuity polish (`apps/web`, `apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `docs`; UX/runtime follow-up 2026-05-02):** tariff-disabled Skills now stay compact instead of dumping the full catalog when `limit=0`; the Skills manager still shows the first page until expanded. The model-visible `files` guidance is also stricter: when the user asks to send/resend/share an existing file, finding it is not enough and `files.send` must happen in the same turn. As a safety net, final assistant text now downgrades local file-style markdown links to plain filename text and appends an honesty correction when no real attachment was delivered. Verification: focused Skills manager, final-delivery-honesty, and native-tool-projection regressions plus API/web/runtime typechecks.

- **Skills panel shadow polish (`apps/web`, `docs`; UI follow-up 2026-05-02):** removed the heavy outer drop shadow from the top `Professional Skills` panel in the Skills manager. The card now relies on its surface fill and border instead of a dark halo, which avoids the hard rectangular edge that was visible in both light and dark themes. Verification: focused Skills manager regression, web typecheck, and changed-file lints.

- **Prompt and action-chip continuity polish (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; UX/prompt follow-up 2026-05-02):** updated the default `Response UI Contract` to stop nudging gender-conflicting Russian openings such as `Понял`, require quick actions to be plain-text user-style imperative requests, and forbid markdown formatting inside action labels. Added explicit Soul guidance for Russian self-reference by assistant gender (`female -> поняла/сделала`, `male -> понял/сделал`, `neutral -> avoid forced gendered phrasing`). Web chat now also normalizes parsed assistant action chips by stripping inline markdown and common assistant-offer prefixes before drafting them into the input, and the mobile chip styling is quieter and more link-like instead of heavy pills. Verification: focused prompt-constructor and chat-message-blocks regressions plus workspace lint, format, and API/web typecheck gates.

- **ADR-079 auto-skill background recheck continuity (`apps/api`, `docs`; routing continuity follow-up 2026-05-02):** fixed the scheduler gap behind the founder's live report that background Skill routing stopped re-checking long chats. `AutoSkillRoutingStateService.shouldRunBackgroundCheck()` now bootstraps immediately when a chat still has `autoSkillRoutingState:null`, so older chats start background routing again after a Skill is enabled or when prior state was never persisted. Persisted `inactive` Skill state now follows the same every-5-message re-check cadence as `active` state instead of getting stuck behind the old `1/2/3 only` gate. Verification: focused API auto-skill-routing-state regression test plus workspace lint, format, and API/web typecheck gates.

- **ADR-074 R2 runtime parallel tool calls (`apps/runtime`, `apps/provider-gateway`, `apps/api`, `docs`; efficiency follow-through 2026-05-02):** implemented safe-parallel execution for the ADR-approved read-only tool set (`web_search`, `web_fetch`, `knowledge_search`, `knowledge_fetch`, `quota_status`) while preserving model-order budget reservation, deterministic `toolHistory` / `toolInvocations`, and serial-only behavior for mutation- or ordering-sensitive tools such as `memory_write`, compaction, scheduling, files/media, browser, `exec`, and `shell`. OpenAI Responses payloads now set `parallel_tool_calls: true` explicitly when tools are present. Prompt/tool guidance now tells the model that independent tool calls may be returned together and run in parallel while dependent chains stay separate. Verification: focused runtime turn-execution, OpenAI provider, native-tool-projection, and prompt-constructor tests plus workspace lint, format, and API/web typecheck gates.

- **Android release CTA polish (`apps/web`, `persai-mobile`, `docs`; mobile release follow-up 2026-05-02):** made the shared APK download/update pill quieter across landing and Assistant Settings by reducing footprint, contrast, and shadow. Native-shell copy now says `Update app` / `Обновить app` instead of the ambiguous `Update` / `Обновить`. Rebuilt and re-exported the Android APK to PersAI web release assets at `1.0.1 (2)`. Verification: Android release export; full gate follows before commit.

- **ADR-079 Skill final activity badge polish (`apps/web`, `docs`; live badge follow-up 2026-05-02):** fixed the remaining live UI gap where `Навык - <emoji>` could disappear when the final visible activity banner was a tool/knowledge/media completion instead of `Ответ готов`. `useChat` now tracks the active Skill badge detail separately from the current activity label and carries it forward across later retrieval, tool, compaction, and runtime-complete activity replacements, keeping long Skill names out of the compact banner. Verification: focused `use-chat` regression and changed-file lints.

- **ADR-081 Files mobile density and cache visibility polish (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; mobile Files follow-up 2026-05-02):** tightened the Assistant Settings Files list for mobile. Useful groups are now collapsed by default and ordered `Media -> Created by assistant -> User files`; cache/history rows are not rendered in the main list. Transient uploaded voice/audio artifacts (`audio/webm`, `audio/ogg`, `audio/opus` from chat/staged upload) are backend-classified as `cache_history` and cleanup-eligible, so `Очистить кэш` appears as a quiet accent banner instead of voice files cluttering Media. File row actions moved onto the filename line at the right to reduce row height. Verification: focused web settings test, API cleanup classification test, API/web typechecks.

- **Mobile setup/recreate continuity polish (`apps/web`, `docs`; Capacitor live follow-up 2026-05-02):** fixed two mobile startup/navigation regressions found during recreate validation. Setup/recreate now registers the active step with the JS back-handler stack, so Android hardware Back moves to the previous wizard step instead of falling through to WebView history/app exit and remounting setup at step 0. `useAppData` no longer treats a rejected assistant fetch as a resolved "no assistant" state, so poor network/VPN startup cannot redirect an already configured user into setup unless the assistant endpoint actually resolves to `null`. Verification: focused setup Back regression, app-data network regression, Assistant Settings test, and web typecheck.

- **ADR-081 Files preview action polish (`apps/web`, `docs`; mobile Files follow-up 2026-05-02):** removed the generic `Open` action from Assistant Settings Files rows because raw canonical file opens are unreliable in Capacitor and not a strong desktop UX. `Download` is now the only universal action, while images and videos get a quiet `Preview` eye action that stays in-app: images reuse the existing lightbox and videos open in a fullscreen controlled player. Verification: focused Assistant Settings Files test and web typecheck.

- **ADR-081 mobile text download encoding polish (`apps/api`, `persai-mobile/android`, `docs`; Capacitor download follow-up 2026-05-02):** fixed Cyrillic text/markdown file contents becoming mojibake after Android Capacitor download while desktop web opened the same file correctly. The Files download endpoint now declares `charset=utf-8` for text-like content and adds a UTF-8 BOM only for forced downloads of plain text/markdown/csv/tsv files so legacy Android viewers can detect encoding after `DownloadManager` saves the file. The Android shell also strips `; charset=...` parameters before calling `DownloadManager.setMimeType`. Verification: focused API media attachment controller regression and API typecheck.

- **ADR-081 mobile Files grouping and cache cleanup (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; mobile Files polish 2026-05-02):** replaced the flat Assistant Settings Files list with a compact mobile-first grouped list. Backend Files responses now carry `fileBucket`, `cleanupEligible`, and `cleanupReason`, so the UI groups into user files, assistant-created files, media, and history/cache without frontend guessing. `История и кэш` is collapsed by default, useful groups stay expanded, rows are denser with compact metadata/action targets, and a quiet `Очистить кэш` action appears only when backend-marked cleanup candidates exist. The cleanup endpoint currently removes only narrow voice-upload cache/history artifacts and marks linked chat attachments deleted; user files, assistant-created/generated files, media uploads, Knowledge, and non-deleted working files are preserved. Verification: API cleanup and auth tests, web API/settings tests, runtime hydration guard, API/web typechecks.

- **ADR-081 delivered file duplicate-link polish (`apps/api`, `apps/web`, `docs`; live smoke follow-up 2026-05-02):** removed the duplicate model-written markdown filename link when the same file is already delivered as a chat attachment card. Final assistant-content post-processing now drops standalone delivered-file markdown link lines and downgrades embedded delivered-file markdown links to plain filename text, while web markdown rendering refuses unsafe/relative filename links so the streaming view cannot navigate/refresh the app. The attachment card remains the single download/open surface. Verification: focused delivery-honesty sanitizer test, web chat-message test, API/web typechecks.

- **ADR-081 Files refresh/delete polish (`apps/api`, `apps/runtime`, `apps/web`, `docs`; live smoke follow-up 2026-05-02):** fixed generated chat file cards losing their active download after refresh by persisting existing runtime-generated `fileRef` links onto `assistant_chat_message_attachments` before history reloads depend on them. Deleting an `AssistantFile` now quietly marks linked chat attachments as `fileDeleted` before the FK nulls, and web chat cards render a disabled `файл удалён` status instead of a broken-looking file card. Runtime hydration still exposes non-deleted prior user/assistant files to the agent as working files with durable `fileRef`, while deleted attachments are filtered so the agent cannot resurrect them. Verification: focused API media delivery test, web chat-message test, runtime context hydration test, API/web/runtime typechecks.

- **ADR-079 Skill badge SSE metadata fix (`apps/web`, `docs`; live badge reliability 2026-05-02):** fixed the real stream-path reason the selected Skill label/icon still did not appear in the chat activity badge.
